title: Chicken Tinga Tacos
steps: Season the chicken thighs with salt. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and brown the chicken on both sides, about 3 minutes per side (it won't be cooked through yet). Remove to a plate.
Add the remaining oil to the skillet and cook the sliced onion until soft and starting to brown, 5–6 minutes. Add the garlic and cook 30 seconds more.
Stir in the chipotles with their sauce, the diced tomatoes, broth, oregano, and cumin. Scrape up any browned bits from the pan.
Return the chicken to the skillet, cover, and simmer over medium-low for 15 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
Move the chicken to a cutting board and shred it with two forks. Stir it back into the sauce and simmer uncovered 5 minutes, until the sauce clings to the chicken. Taste for salt.
Warm the tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame. Fill with the tinga and top with cilantro, cotija, and a squeeze of lime.
notes: Leftover chipotles freeze well in a zip bag — flatten it so you can snap off one pepper at a time.

title: Coconut Curry Noodle Soup
steps: Soak or cook the rice noodles according to the package, rinse with cool water, and set aside.
Heat the oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add the curry paste, ginger, and garlic and fry for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Whisk in the coconut milk and let it bubble for 2 minutes, then add the broth, fish sauce, and brown sugar. Bring to a simmer.
Add the sliced chicken and bell pepper and simmer gently for 6–8 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
Take the pot off the heat and stir in the lime juice. Taste — it should be salty, sweet, and tangy; adjust with more fish sauce or lime.
Divide the noodles between bowls, ladle the soup over, and top generously with cilantro.
notes: Shrimp or tofu swap in easily for the chicken.

title: Quick Chana Masala
steps: Start the rice cooking however you usually do.
Heat the oil in a deep skillet over medium heat. Cook the onion with a pinch of salt until golden at the edges, 6–8 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, and tomato paste and cook 1 minute, stirring. Add the garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cayenne and toast for 30 seconds.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and ½ cup water. Simmer 5 minutes, until slightly thickened.
Add the chickpeas and simmer 10 minutes, mashing a few against the side of the pan to thicken the sauce. Add splashes of water if it gets too thick.
Off the heat, stir in the lemon juice and half the cilantro. Salt to taste. Serve over rice with the rest of the cilantro on top.
notes: Even better the next day — make a double batch.

title: Weeknight Bolognese
steps: Heat the olive oil in a wide pot over medium heat. Cook the onion, carrot, and celery with a big pinch of salt until soft, about 8 minutes.
Push the vegetables aside, raise the heat to medium-high, and add the beef. Break it up and cook until no longer pink, then keep going until some of it browns, about 8 minutes total.
Stir in the garlic and tomato paste and cook 2 minutes, until the paste darkens a shade.
Add the crushed tomatoes, milk, oregano, and bay leaf. Simmer gently, partly covered, for 30 minutes, stirring now and then. Add a splash of water if it gets thicker than you like.
Meanwhile cook the spaghetti in well-salted water to al dente; reserve a cup of pasta water before draining.
Fish out the bay leaf. Toss the pasta with the sauce and parmesan, loosening with pasta water as needed. Season and serve with more parmesan.
notes: The milk sounds odd but makes the sauce silky — don't skip it. Sauce freezes well.

title: Fluffy Buttermilk Pancakes

ingredients.prep: melted, plus more for the pan

steps: Whisk the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl.
In another bowl, whisk the buttermilk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla.
Pour the wet into the dry and stir just until no dry streaks remain — lumps are fine. Let the batter rest 5 minutes.
Heat a griddle or nonstick skillet over medium heat and rub with a little butter.
Scoop ⅓-cup rounds and cook until bubbles pop on the surface and the edges look set, 2–3 minutes. Flip and cook 1–2 minutes more.
Keep warm in a 200°F oven while you finish the batch. Serve with butter and maple syrup.
notes: Extra pancakes freeze well — reheat in the toaster.

title: Buttermilk Oven-Fried Chicken
steps: Stir the buttermilk and hot sauce together in a bowl or zip bag, add the chicken, and marinate at least 30 minutes (overnight is better).
Heat the oven to 425°F. Set a wire rack on a foil-lined sheet pan and oil it lightly.
Mix the panko, parmesan, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper in a shallow dish, then drizzle in the melted butter and toss until the crumbs look like damp sand.
Lift each piece of chicken out of the buttermilk, let the excess drip off, and press firmly into the crumbs to coat all over. Set on the rack.
Bake 35–40 minutes, until deep golden and an instant-read thermometer hits 175°F at the bone.
Rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: The leftover marinade buttermilk gets tossed, so this pairs nicely with pancakes the same week to finish the carton.

title: Same-Day Rustic Dutch Oven Loaf
steps: Activate Yeast: Combine warm water, sugar, and yeast in the KitchenAid bowl. Stir briefly. Let sit 5 to 10 minutes until foamy. If it does not foam, your yeast is dead or water was too hot. Start over.

Mix and Knead: Add flour, salt, and olive oil to the bowl. Fit the dough hook. Mix on Speed 1 for 2 minutes until a shaggy dough forms. Increase to Speed 2 and knead 8 to 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
First Rise: Lightly oil the bowl and turn the dough to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp towel. Rise in a warm spot 1.5 to 2 hours until doubled.
Shape: Lightly flour a clean surface. Tip the dough out. Gently fold the edges toward the center, flip seam-side down, and cup your hands around it to tighten into a round ball.
Second Rise: Line a bowl with a well-floured towel (or use a floured proofing basket). Place dough seam-side up. Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake: Place your Dutch oven (lid on) in the oven and preheat to 245 C / 475 F for at least 30 minutes.
Carefully remove the Dutch oven. Flip your dough in, seam-side down. Score the top. Put the lid on.
Bake covered 25 minutes. Remove lid. Bake another 15 to 20 minutes until deep golden brown.
Cool on a wire rack at least 45 minutes before cutting.
notes: Tips: The more you score, the more the loaf opens up. A single bold slash gives the classic ear. If your kitchen is cold, rise in the oven with just the light on. For extra flavor, add 1 teaspoon of apple cider vinegar to the water.

title: Long-Ferment "Fake Sourdough"
steps: Combine all ingredients in the KitchenAid bowl. Dough hook, Speed 1 for 3 minutes, Speed 2 for 8 to 10 minutes. Dough will be slightly stickier than standard. That is fine.
Lightly oil the bowl. Cover tightly with plastic wrap. Refrigerate 12 to 18 hours (overnight is ideal).
Next day: remove from refrigerator. Let sit at room temperature 1.5 to 2 hours.
Shape into a round. Place seam-side up in a well-floured towel-lined bowl. Cover. Rise 1 hour.
Preheat Dutch oven at 245 C / 475 F for 30 minutes. Flip dough in, score top, bake covered 25 minutes, uncovered 15 to 20 minutes.
Cool completely before slicing, at least 1 hour.
notes: Tips: The longer the cold ferment (up to 72 hours), the tangier the bread. Adding a tablespoon of whole wheat flour in place of some bread flour deepens flavor.

title: No-Knead Overnight Dutch Oven Loaf
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, yeast, and salt. Add water. Stir with a wooden spoon until no dry flour remains. Cover tightly. Leave at room temperature 12 to 18 hours.
The dough will have risen and look bubbly. Flour a work surface well. Scrape out the dough (very sticky). Flour your hands. Fold dough over itself a few times. Shape loosely into a ball.
Place on well-floured parchment paper. Dust the top with flour. Cover loosely. Rest 1 to 2 hours until puffy.
Place Dutch oven in oven. Preheat to 245 C / 475 F for 30 minutes.
Score the top. Lift parchment and lower into Dutch oven. Lid on. Bake 30 minutes covered, 15 minutes uncovered.
Cool at least 1 hour.
notes: Tips: No-knead dough is intentionally sticky. Do not add more flour. Parchment paper makes transferring to the hot Dutch oven much easier and safer.

title: Classic Sandwich Loaf
steps: Make Tangzhong: Combine 28g bread flour (taken from the 480g total) and 140g warm whole milk (taken from the 240g milk total) in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until it becomes a thick glossy paste (about 60 to 90 seconds total). Cover surface with plastic wrap. Cool to room temperature.
Make Dough: Warm the remaining 100g milk and 60g water together (38 to 43 C). Combine with yeast and sugar. Stir. Let sit 5 minutes until foamy.

Add the remaining 452g flour, salt, butter, egg, and cooled tangzhong to the KitchenAid bowl. Pour in the yeast liquid. Dough hook, Speed 1 for 2 minutes, Speed 2 for 8 to 10 minutes. Dough should be smooth, soft, and slightly tacky.
Oil bowl, cover, rise 1 to 1.5 hours until doubled.
Punch down gently. On a lightly floured surface, pat into a rectangle the same width as your pan. Roll tightly into a log. Pinch seam. Place seam-side down in a greased 9x5 pan.
Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes until dough crowns about 2.5 cm / 1 inch above the rim.
Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Bake 30 to 35 minutes until deep golden. Internal temp 93 C / 200 F.
Remove from pan immediately. Cool on a rack at least 1 hour.
notes: Tips: Brush the top with melted butter right out of the oven for a softer, shiny crust. Slices cleanest the next day.

title: Soft Dinner Rolls
steps: Make tangzhong: 30g bread flour (from the 480g total) + 150g warm milk (from the 240g milk total), microwave method. Cool.
Combine the remaining 90g warm milk, 60g water, yeast, and sugar. Let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
Add the remaining 450g flour, salt, butter, eggs, and cooled tangzhong. Dough hook, Speed 1 for 2 minutes, Speed 2 for 8 to 10 minutes. Smooth and elastic.
Oil bowl, cover, rise 1 to 1.5 hours until doubled.
Divide into 12 equal pieces (about 80 to 85g each). Shape each into a tight ball by pulling the top smooth and pinching the seam underneath.
Arrange in a greased 9x13 pan with rolls touching. Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes until puffy.
Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Bake 18 to 22 minutes until golden.
Brush immediately with the 30g melted butter. Serve warm.
notes: Tips: Tight shaping is the key to a nice round top. Rolls reheat well covered with foil at 175 C / 350 F for 10 minutes.

title: Burger Buns, Soft Brioche Style
steps: Make tangzhong: 28g bread flour (from the 450g total) + 140g warm milk (from the 180g milk total). Cool.
Combine the remaining 40g warm milk, 60g water, yeast, sugar, and honey. Let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
Add the remaining 422g flour, salt, butter, 1 egg, and cooled tangzhong. Dough hook, Speed 1 for 2 minutes, Speed 2 for 10 minutes.
Oil bowl, cover, rise 1 to 1.5 hours until doubled.
Divide into 8 pieces (about 97g each). Shape into smooth tight balls. Place on parchment-lined baking sheet.
Gently flatten each to a disc about 9 cm / 3.5 inches wide. Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Beat the remaining egg with a tablespoon of water. Brush buns. Add sesame seeds if using.
Bake 15 to 18 minutes until deep golden. Cool completely before slicing.
notes: Tips: Flatten more than you think you need to. They puff up a lot. Brushing twice (before baking and halfway through) gives a beautiful gloss.

title: Sturdy Sandwich Buns

ingredients.item: everything bagel seasoning or poppy seeds

steps: Make tangzhong: 25g bread flour (from the 500g total) + 125g water (from the 300g water total). Cool.
Combine the remaining 175g warm water, yeast, and sugar. Let sit 5 minutes.
Add the remaining 475g flour, salt, olive oil, and cooled tangzhong. Dough hook, Speed 1 for 2 minutes, Speed 2 for 10 minutes.
Oil bowl, cover, rise 1 to 1.5 hours.
Divide into 8 pieces (about 104g each). Shape into tight balls, flatten to discs 10 cm / 4 inches wide. Place on parchment-lined baking sheet.
Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Preheat oven to 205 C / 400 F. Place a small pan of water on the bottom rack (creates steam for a better crust).
Egg wash the tops. Add toppings if using. Bake 18 to 22 minutes.
Cool completely before slicing.
notes: Tips: The steam pan is the trick to a good crust on lean buns. These hold up much better for anything saucy or wet.

title: Roasted Garlic and Rosemary Loaf
steps: Roast the Garlic: Cut the top off the garlic head to expose the cloves. Drizzle with olive oil. Wrap in foil. Roast at 200 C / 400 F for 40 to 45 minutes until soft and golden. Cool. Squeeze and mash the cloves.

Make the Dough: Activate yeast in warm water with sugar, 5 minutes. Add flour, salt, olive oil, rosemary, and pepper. Dough hook, Speed 2 for 7 minutes. Add roasted garlic paste. Speed 2 for 2 to 3 more minutes.
Oil bowl, rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Shape into round. Proof in floured bowl 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake in Dutch oven at 245 C / 475 F: covered 25 minutes, uncovered 15 to 20 minutes.
Cool 45 minutes.
notes: Tips: Roast two heads of garlic at once and freeze the extra. Add 60g of shredded Parmesan with the garlic for a cheesy garlic version.

title: Cheddar Jalapeño Loaf
steps: Make tangzhong: 25g bread flour (from the 450g total) + 125g water (from the 300g water total). Cool.
Activate yeast in the remaining 175g warm water with sugar.
Add the remaining 425g flour, salt, olive oil, garlic powder, and cooled tangzhong. Dough hook, Speed 2 for 8 minutes.
Add cheddar and jalapeño. Speed 1 for 1 to 2 minutes to fold in gently.
Oil bowl, rise 1.5 to 2 hours.
Shape into a log. Place in greased 9x5 pan. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 35 to 40 minutes.
Cool 45 minutes.
notes: Tips: Cubed cheese gives visible pockets. Shredded cheese fully melts into the dough. Add 50g of pickled jalapeños for extra tang.

title: Olive and Sun-Dried Tomato Loaf

ingredients.prep: or use oil from the sun-dried tomato jar

steps: Activate yeast. Mix flour, salt, olive oil, herbs, and pepper with liquid. Knead Speed 2 for 8 minutes.
Add olives and sun-dried tomatoes. Speed 1 for 1 to 2 minutes.
Oil bowl, rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Shape. Proof in floured bowl 45 to 60 minutes.
Dutch oven bake: 245 C / 475 F, covered 25 minutes, uncovered 15 to 20 minutes.
Cool fully.
notes: Tips: Pat olives and tomatoes dry before adding. Add 60g of crumbled feta for a more Mediterranean version.

title: Everything Bagel Pull-Apart Loaf
steps: Make tangzhong: 25g bread flour (from the 450g total) + 125g water (from the 240g water total). Cool.
Activate yeast in the remaining 115g warm water with sugar.
Add the remaining 425g flour, salt, butter, egg, and cooled tangzhong. Dough hook, Speed 2 for 8 to 10 minutes. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours.
Mix cream cheese with garlic and chives.
Roll dough to a rectangle about 35x45 cm / 14x18 inches. Brush with melted butter. Spread cream cheese mixture across the surface.
Cut the rectangle into 4 long strips. Stack the strips. Cut the stack into 6 to 7 sections.
Stand sections cut-side up in a greased 9x5 loaf pan. Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Brush top with melted butter. Coat heavily with everything bagel seasoning.
Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 30 to 35 minutes.
Cool 15 minutes before pulling apart.
notes: Tips: Use a sharp knife or pizza cutter for cutting the layered strips cleanly. Great warm, even better the next day toasted.

title: Cinnamon Swirl Loaf
steps: Make tangzhong: 30g bread flour (from the 480g total) + 150g warm milk (from the 240g milk total). Cool.

Activate yeast in the remaining 90g warm milk with sugar. Mix all dough ingredients (remaining 450g flour, salt, eggs, butter, vanilla) including cooled tangzhong. Knead Speed 2 for 9 to 10 minutes. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours.
Mix cinnamon filling (100g brown sugar, 15g cinnamon, 1g salt, 30g very soft butter) into a thick paste.
Roll dough to 40x25 cm / 16x10 inches. Spread filling to all edges. Roll up tightly from the short end. Pinch seam. Place seam-side down in greased 9x5 pan.
Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes. Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 35 to 40 minutes. Cover loosely with foil if it browns too fast.
Cool in pan 15 minutes, then on rack. Beat the optional cream cheese glaze ingredients (85g cream cheese, 100g powdered sugar, 30g heavy cream or milk, 3g vanilla, pinch of salt) and drizzle over.
notes: Tips: The soft butter in the filling is critical. It needs to spread, not tear. For extra cinnamon punch, dust the outside of the rolled log with more cinnamon before placing in the pan.

title: Lemon Swirl Loaf with Lemon Glaze

ingredients.prep: zest of 2 lemons, add to dough
zest of 1 additional lemon, for filling

steps: Make tangzhong: 30g bread flour (from the 480g total) + 150g warm milk (from the 240g milk total). Cool.

Make dough adding lemon zest (of 2 lemons) with the other ingredients (remaining 450g flour, remaining 90g warm milk, salt, sugar, butter, eggs, vanilla) and cooled tangzhong. Knead Speed 2 for 9 to 10 minutes. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours.
Mix lemon filling ingredients (160g lemon curd, 30g softened butter, 50g sugar, zest of 1 lemon) into a spreadable mixture.
Roll dough to 40x25 cm / 16x10 inches. Spread lemon filling. Roll tightly from the short end. Place in greased 9x5 pan.
Rise 45 to 60 minutes. Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 35 to 40 minutes.
Cool 20 minutes. Mix glaze (150g powdered sugar, 30 to 45g fresh lemon juice, zest of 1 lemon) and pour over. Let set before slicing.
notes: Tips: Do not skip the zest in the dough. It makes the lemon flavor pervasive throughout, not just in the filling.

title: Chai Spice Swirl Loaf
steps: Make tangzhong: 30g bread flour (from the 480g total) + 150g warm milk (from the 240g milk total). Cool.

Make dough, adding cardamom and cinnamon to the dough. Include the remaining 450g flour, remaining 90g warm milk, salt, sugar, butter, eggs, vanilla, and cooled tangzhong. Knead Speed 2 for 9 to 10 minutes. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours.
Mix all filling spices with brown sugar and soft butter (100g brown sugar, 10g cinnamon, 5g cardamom, 3g ginger, 1g cloves, 1g black pepper, 30g very soft butter) into a thick paste.
Roll to 40x25 cm / 16x10 inches. Spread filling. Roll tightly. Place in greased 9x5 pan.
Rise 45 to 60 minutes. Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 35 to 40 minutes.
Cool. Mix glaze (120g powdered sugar, 30g heavy cream, 5g vanilla, pinch of cardamom) and drizzle.
notes: Tips: The black pepper in the filling is authentic to chai. Do not skip it. Replace some of the milk with brewed strong chai tea for an even deeper flavor.

title: Chocolate Babka Swirl Loaf
steps: Make tangzhong: 25g bread flour (from the 420g total) + 125g warm milk (from the 145g milk total). Cool.

Activate yeast. Mix all dough ingredients (remaining 395g flour, remaining 20g warm milk, 80g water, salt, sugar, eggs, vanilla) including cooled tangzhong. Knead Speed 2 for 5 minutes. Add the 85g butter in small pieces, knead 8 to 10 more minutes. Rise 1.5 to 2 hours or refrigerate overnight.
Chocolate Filling: Melt the 85g butter and 170g chocolate together. Stir in powdered sugar, cocoa, and salt. Cool until thick but spreadable.
Assemble: Roll dough to 45x35 cm / 18x14 inches. Spread chocolate filling. Roll up from the long end into a log.
Cut the log in half lengthwise with a serrated knife, exposing the layers. Cross the two halves over each other (twist them), cut sides facing up. Place in greased 9x5 pan.
Cover. Rise 1 hour.
Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 35 to 45 minutes. Cover with foil if top browns too fast.
Boil the 60g water and 60g sugar together 2 minutes. Brush all over the hot loaf immediately out of the oven.
Cool completely before slicing.
notes: Tips: Use a serrated knife for cutting the log lengthwise. Add a teaspoon of espresso powder to the filling to deepen the chocolate flavor.

title: Orange Cranberry Loaf

ingredients.prep: soaked in warm water 10 minutes, drained

steps: Make tangzhong: 25g bread flour (from the 450g total) + 125g gently warmed orange juice (from the 200g orange juice total). Cool.
Activate yeast in the remaining 75g warm orange juice and 60g water with 10g sugar (from the 40g sugar total).
Mix the remaining 425g flour, remaining 30g sugar, salt, butter, eggs, zest, and cooled tangzhong. Knead Speed 2 for 8 to 9 minutes.
Add cranberries. Speed 1 for 1 to 2 minutes.
Oil bowl, rise 1 to 1.5 hours. Shape into log, place in greased 9x5 pan. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 35 to 40 minutes. Cool completely. Add glaze (150g powdered sugar, 30 to 40g fresh orange juice, zest of 1 orange).
notes: Tips: Using orange juice in the tangzhong doubles down on citrus flavor throughout the loaf. Works with dried cherries, blueberries, or apricots in place of cranberries.

title: Cinnamon Rolls with Cream Cheese Frosting
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 35g of the bread flour and 175g of the whole milk in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until it becomes a thick glossy paste. Cover the surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (These amounts come out of the total bread flour and milk listed; do not add extra.)

Activate yeast in the remaining 65g warm milk and 80g warm water with 10g of the sugar. Mix the remaining dough ingredients (remaining bread flour, yeast, salt, remaining sugar, butter, eggs, vanilla) including the cooled tangzhong. Knead with the dough hook on Speed 2 for 10 minutes. Rise 1.5 hours.
Assemble: Roll the dough to a large rectangle, 50x35 cm / 20x14 inches.
Mix the filling (brown sugar, ground cinnamon, fine salt, very soft butter) into a thick paste. Spread to all edges.
Roll up very tightly from the long side. Cut into 12 equal pieces using a serrated knife or dental floss.
Arrange in a greased 9x13 pan. Cover. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours until very puffy.
Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 25 to 30 minutes.
Frosting: Beat cream cheese and butter until completely smooth. Add powdered sugar, cream, vanilla, and salt. Beat until fluffy.
Spread generously over hot rolls right out of the oven.
notes: For overnight rolls: cover the pan after shaping and refrigerate. Pull out in the morning, let warm 1 hour, then bake. Do not overbake; pull when the center rolls are just set. More frosting is always correct.

title: Caramelized Onion and Gruyere Loaf
steps: Caramelize onions: Cook the sliced onions in butter and olive oil with 5g salt and 5g sugar over medium-low heat, 40 to 60 minutes until deeply golden. Cool completely.

Make tangzhong: combine 25g of the bread flour and 125g of the water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth and microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until thick and glossy. Cover the surface with plastic wrap and cool. (These amounts come out of the total bread flour and water listed; do not add extra.)
Activate yeast in the remaining 175g warm water with 5g sugar. Mix the remaining flour, salt, olive oil, thyme, and cooled tangzhong. Knead with the dough hook on Speed 2 for 8 minutes.
Add the cooled caramelized onions and Gruyere. Speed 1 for 2 minutes.
Oil the bowl, cover, rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Shape into a log. Place in a greased 9x5 pan. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 35 to 42 minutes. Cool 1 hour.
notes: Caramelized onions can be made days ahead and stored in the fridge. Gruyere can be replaced with Swiss, Emmental, or Comté.

title: Bacon, Cheddar, and Chive Loaf
steps: Cook bacon until crispy. Drain. Cool and crumble. Reserve 1 tablespoon of bacon fat.

Make tangzhong: combine 25g of the bread flour and 125g of the water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth and microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until thick and glossy. Cover the surface and cool. (These amounts come out of the total bread flour and water listed; do not add extra.)

Activate yeast in the remaining 175g warm water with the sugar. Mix the remaining flour, salt, reserved bacon fat, smoked paprika, pepper, and cooled tangzhong. Knead with the dough hook on Speed 2 for 8 minutes.
Add the bacon, cheddar, and chives. Speed 1 for 1 to 2 minutes.
Oil the bowl, rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Shape into a log, place in a greased 9x5 pan. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 35 to 42 minutes. Cool 45 minutes.
notes: The reserved bacon fat in the dough adds incredible depth of flavor.

title: Dark Chocolate and Dried Cherry Loaf

ingredients.prep: soaked in warm water 10 minutes, drained

steps: Make tangzhong: combine 25g of the bread flour and 125g of the whole milk in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth and microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until thick and glossy. Cover the surface and cool. (These amounts come out of the total bread flour and milk listed; do not add extra.)

Activate yeast. Mix all dough ingredients (remaining flour, yeast, salt, sugar, remaining milk, butter, eggs, vanilla, and optional cocoa powder) including the cooled tangzhong. Knead with the dough hook on Speed 2 for 9 minutes.
Add the chocolate and cherries. Speed 1 for 1 to 2 minutes.
Oil the bowl, rise 1 to 1.5 hours. Shape into a log, place in a greased 9x5 pan. Rise 45 to 60 minutes.
Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 35 to 40 minutes. Cool fully.
notes: A light dusting of cocoa powder on the greased pan before placing the dough gives a chocolatey crust.

title: Cardamom and Almond Swirl Loaf
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 25g of the bread flour and 125g of the whole milk in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth and microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until thick and glossy. Cover the surface and cool. (These amounts come out of the total bread flour and milk listed; do not add extra.)

Make the dough with the cardamom added and the cooled tangzhong included (remaining flour, yeast, salt, sugar, remaining milk, butter, eggs, vanilla, 5g cardamom). Knead with the dough hook on Speed 2 for 9 minutes. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours.
Mix all filling ingredients (almond flour, sugar, butter, 8g cardamom, almond extract, egg white) into a smooth paste.
Roll dough to 40x25 cm / 16x10 inches. Spread filling. Roll up tightly from the short end. Place in a greased 9x5 pan.
Rise 45 to 60 minutes. Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 35 to 40 minutes.
Cool. Mix glaze (powdered sugar, milk or cream, almond extract), pour over, and scatter sliced almonds.
notes: This loaf is excellent the next day after the flavors meld. Almond extract is potent; start with less if you are unfamiliar with it.

title: No-Knead Overnight Focaccia
steps: Night before: whisk flour, yeast, and salt. Add water and 40g olive oil. Stir until all flour is incorporated. Cover. Refrigerate overnight.
Next day: pour 40g olive oil into a 9x13 baking pan. Tip cold dough in. Gently stretch toward corners. Let rest 10 minutes. Stretch again. Repeat until it fills the pan.
Cover with plastic wrap. Rise at room temperature 2 to 3 hours until very puffy and bubbly.
Preheat oven to 230 C / 450 F.
Drizzle extra olive oil over top. Oil your fingers and dimple the dough aggressively all over, pressing to the pan bottom.
Scatter toppings (choose your favorites: cherry tomatoes, rosemary, olives, roasted garlic, red onion). Sprinkle generously with flaky sea salt.
Bake 22 to 26 minutes until deep golden brown.
Cool in pan 10 minutes. Slide out and cool on a rack.
notes: The dimpling step is essential; it creates craters that hold the olive oil and toppings. Check the bottom by tilting the pan; it should be golden brown and crispy. Focaccia reheats well at 190 C / 375 F for 10 minutes.

title: No-Knead Olive Oil and Herb Loaf
steps: Whisk flour, yeast, salt, and all herbs and spices. Add water and olive oil. Stir until no dry flour remains. Cover tightly. Rest 12 to 18 hours at room temperature.
Flour a work surface. Scrape dough out (sticky). Flour your hands. Fold dough over itself 4 to 6 times. Shape loosely into a ball.
Place on floured parchment. Dust top with flour. Cover loosely. Rest 1.5 to 2 hours.
Place Dutch oven in oven. Preheat to 245 C / 475 F for 30 minutes.
Score the dough. Lift parchment and lower into Dutch oven. Bake covered 30 minutes, uncovered 15 to 20 minutes.
Cool 1 hour.
notes: No-knead dough is much wetter than kneaded dough. Resist adding more flour.

title: No-Knead Cheesy Pull-Apart Rolls
steps: Mix flour, yeast, salt, sugar, water, and olive oil until combined. Cover. Refrigerate overnight.
Next day, let dough sit at room temperature 1 hour.
Flour a work surface well. Divide into 9 pieces. Flatten each, place cheese and garlic in center, fold edges up, pinch into a rough ball.
Place in a greased 9-inch round pan. Rolls touching. Cover. Rise 1.5 to 2 hours.
Brush with garlic herb butter (melted butter mixed with dried Italian herbs). Sprinkle remaining cheese on top.
Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 25 to 30 minutes. Serve warm.
notes: Cheese that melts out creates crispy bits on the bottom.

title: No-Knead Caramelized Onion Bread
steps: Caramelize onions in olive oil over medium-low heat 45 to 60 minutes until deep golden. Cool completely.
Mix flour, yeast, salt, water, olive oil, thyme, and pepper until combined. Fold in cooled onions. Cover. Rest 12 to 18 hours.

Shape and proof as in the No-Knead Overnight Loaf: flour a work surface well, scrape out the dough, fold it over itself a few times, and shape loosely into a ball. Place on well-floured parchment, dust the top with flour, cover loosely, and rest 1 to 2 hours until puffy.
Bake in Dutch oven at 245 C / 475 F: covered 30 minutes, uncovered 15 to 20 minutes.
Cool 1 hour.
notes: Caramelized onions can be made and refrigerated up to 5 days ahead.

title: No-Knead Cinnamon Raisin Bread

ingredients.prep: soaked in warm water 10 minutes, then drained

steps: Mix all ingredients until combined. Fold in raisins. Cover. Refrigerate overnight.
Next day, let warm at room temperature 1 hour.
Pour into a well-greased 9x5 pan. Smooth the top.
Cover. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours until dough rises about 2 cm above the pan rim.
Bake at 175 C / 350 F for 40 to 45 minutes. Internal temp 93 C / 200 F.
Cool in pan 15 minutes, then on rack.
notes: Soaking raisins prevents them from pulling moisture from the dough. Brush the top with a sugar-water glaze (equal parts) hot out of the oven for a shiny top.

title: Classic Beer Bread

ingredients.prep: or 360g all-purpose flour + 10g baking powder + 6g salt
one standard bottle or can, room temperature

steps: Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Grease a 9x5 pan.
Combine flour, sugar, and beer. Stir until just combined. Batter will be thick and rough.
Pour into pan. Drizzle melted butter over the top.
Bake 45 to 55 minutes until golden and a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool 15 minutes before slicing.
notes: Darker beer gives deeper, richer flavor. A can of hard cider works beautifully in place of beer. Add 120g shredded cheddar and 5g garlic powder for a cheesy version.

title: Cheddar and Herb Batter Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Grease a 9x5 pan.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, pepper, sugar, garlic powder, and herbs.
Whisk eggs, milk, sour cream, and oil.
Fold wet into dry until just combined. Fold in cheddar and chives.
Pour into pan. Sprinkle extra cheese on top if desired.
Bake 45 to 55 minutes until golden and a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool 15 minutes.
notes: Do not overmix. A few lumps are fine.

title: Sun-Dried Tomato and Olive Batter Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Grease pan.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, pepper, and herbs.
Whisk eggs, milk, and olive oil.
Fold wet into dry until just combined. Fold in tomatoes, olives, Parmesan, and garlic.
Pour into pan. Top with extra Parmesan.
Bake 45 to 55 minutes. Cool 15 minutes.
notes: Pat tomatoes and olives dry before adding to avoid excess oil.

title: Caramelized Onion Batter Bread
steps: Caramelize onions in butter with sugar over medium-low heat, 40 to 50 minutes. Cool completely.
Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Grease pan.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, pepper, and thyme.
Whisk eggs, milk, and oil.
Fold wet into dry. Fold in cooled onions and cheese.
Pour into pan. Top with extra cheese.
Bake 50 to 60 minutes. Cool 15 minutes.
notes: Make the onions a day ahead to save time.

title: Jalapeño Cheddar Batter Bread

ingredients.prep: 2 diced (seeded for mild, unseeded for hot), 1 sliced into rounds for topping

steps: Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Grease pan.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and cumin.
Whisk eggs, milk, sour cream, and butter.
Fold wet into dry. Fold in cheddar and diced jalapeño.
Pour into pan. Arrange jalapeño rounds on top. Add extra cheese.
Bake 48 to 55 minutes. Cool 15 minutes.
notes: Wear gloves when dicing jalapeños.

title: Lemon Poppy Seed Loaf
steps: Preheat oven to 175 C / 350 F. Grease pan.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, sugar, and poppy seeds.
Whisk eggs, sour cream, oil, lemon zest (from 3 lemons), lemon juice, and vanilla.
Fold wet into dry until just combined.
Pour into pan. Bake 55 to 65 minutes.
While still hot, mix the soak glaze (powdered sugar, 40g lemon juice, zest of 1 lemon) and pour slowly over the top. It soaks in.
Cool in pan 20 minutes, then on a rack.
notes: More zest means more lemon flavor. Do not be shy.

title: Orange Cranberry Quick Bread

ingredients.prep: fresh or frozen (or dried cranberries soaked in warm water)
for optional orange glaze, range 30 to 40g

steps: Preheat oven to 175 C / 350 F. Grease pan.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar.
Whisk eggs, milk, oil, orange zest, juice, and vanilla.
Fold wet into dry until mostly combined. Fold in cranberries. Do not overmix.
Pour into pan. Bake 55 to 65 minutes until golden.
Cool in pan 15 minutes. Drizzle glaze if using.
Tip: If using fresh cranberries, halve the larger ones.

title: Cinnamon Swirl Quick Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 175 C / 350 F. Grease pan.
Mix cinnamon swirl (60g brown sugar, 10g ground cinnamon, 10g melted butter) into a thick paste. Set aside.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar. Whisk eggs, milk, oil, vanilla, and sour cream.
Fold wet into dry until just combined.
Pour half the batter into pan. Spread all the cinnamon swirl. Pour remaining batter on top.
Use a knife to zigzag through the batter to swirl.
Bake 55 to 65 minutes. Cool completely before slicing.
Tip: Sprinkle extra cinnamon sugar on top before baking for a crackle top.

title: Chocolate Chip Snacking Loaf
steps: Preheat oven to 175 C / 350 F. Grease pan.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, cocoa, and sugar.
Whisk eggs, sour cream, oil, vanilla, and coffee.
Fold wet into dry. Fold in most chips.
Pour into pan. Scatter remaining chips on top.
Bake 55 to 65 minutes. Cool completely.
Tip: Espresso powder (5g) added to the dry ingredients deepens the chocolate flavor.

title: Chai and Brown Sugar Quick Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 175 C / 350 F. Grease pan.
Whisk all dry ingredients including all spices. Whisk wet ingredients.
Fold wet into dry until just combined.
Pour into pan. Bake 55 to 65 minutes.
Cool. Mix glaze (150g powdered sugar, 30g milk, 3g vanilla extract, 2g ground cinnamon, 1g cardamom) and drizzle over.
Tip: Brewed chai tea in place of milk gives a deeper, more layered spice flavor.

title: Blueberry Lemon Quick Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 175 C / 350 F. Grease pan.
Toss blueberries in 10g flour to prevent sinking.
Rub lemon zest into sugar until fragrant. Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. Add lemon sugar.
Whisk eggs, milk, sour cream, oil, lemon juice, and vanilla.
Fold wet into dry. Gently fold in blueberries.
Pour into pan. Bake 55 to 65 minutes. Cool completely. Drizzle glaze (100g powdered sugar, 30g fresh lemon juice).
Tip: Use frozen blueberries frozen (not thawed) to prevent the batter turning purple.

title: Dark Chocolate and Espresso Quick Bread
steps: Preheat oven to 175 C / 350 F. Grease pan.
Whisk flour, cocoa, baking powder, salt, sugar, and espresso powder.
Whisk eggs, sour cream, oil, vanilla, and coffee.
Fold wet into dry. Fold in most chips.
Pour into pan. Scatter remaining chips on top. Bake 55 to 65 minutes. Cool completely.
Melt chocolate and cream together. Pour over cooled loaf. Scatter flaky salt.
Tip: This loaf gets better on day 2 as the flavors deepen.

title: Classic Anadama Batter Bread
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Pour boiling water over cornmeal in a large bowl. Stir. Cool to lukewarm.
Stir in molasses, butter, and salt until butter melts.
When lukewarm, sprinkle in yeast. Stir. Sit 5 minutes.
Beat in egg and cooled tangzhong. Add remaining flour (260g). Beat vigorously 2 minutes until smooth.
Pour into greased 9x5 pan. Smooth top. Cover. Rise 1 to 1.5 hours until above the rim.
Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Bake 35 to 40 minutes. Cool in pan 10 minutes, then on rack.
Tip: Molasses intensity varies by brand. Regular baking molasses is milder than blackstrap.

title: English Muffin Batter Bread
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 30g of the all-purpose flour with 150g of the water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 30g flour and 150g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Combine remaining warm water and milk with yeast and sugar. Sit 5 minutes.
Whisk remaining flour (570g) and salt. Stir in yeast liquid, baking soda, and cooled tangzhong. Beat vigorously 2 minutes.
Grease two 9x5 pans. Dust each with cornmeal.
Divide batter equally. Smooth tops. Dust tops with more cornmeal.
Cover. Rise 45 to 60 minutes until risen above the rim.
Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Bake 25 to 30 minutes. Remove from pans immediately. Cool completely.
Tip: The cornmeal on the outside is authentic and gives the English muffin texture.

title: Honey Oat Batter Bread
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the warm water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Soak oats in 120g of the warm water for 10 minutes.
Combine remaining warm water, yeast, and honey. Sit 5 minutes.
Add soaked oats, remaining flour (340g), salt, butter, egg, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 to 3 minutes.
Pour into greased pan. Smooth top. Sprinkle oats on top, press lightly.
Cover. Rise 1.5 to 2 hours until above the rim.
Preheat oven to 190 C / 375 F. Bake 35 to 40 minutes. Cool in pan 10 minutes, then on rack.
Tip: Drizzle a thin line of honey across the top before baking for a shiny crust.

title: Cheddar and Roasted Garlic Batter Bread

ingredients.prep: roasted (cut top off head, drizzle with olive oil, wrap in foil, roast at 200 C / 400 F for 40 to 45 minutes until soft, cool, squeeze and mash)

steps: Roast garlic and cool. Squeeze and mash cloves.

Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the warm water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Combine remaining warm water, yeast, and sugar. Sit 5 minutes.
Add remaining flour (380g), salt, garlic powder, pepper, olive oil, egg, mashed garlic, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 minutes.
Fold in most of the cheddar. Pour into greased pan. Scatter remaining cheddar on top.
Cover. Rise 1.5 to 2 hours.
Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 35 to 42 minutes. Cool in pan 10 minutes.
Tip: The cheese on top forms a crispy crust.

title: Sun-Dried Tomato and Basil Batter Bread
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the warm water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Activate yeast in remaining warm water with sugar.
Add remaining flour (380g), salt, olive oil, egg, garlic, oregano, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 minutes.
Fold in tomatoes, basil, and Parmesan.
Pour into greased pan. Top with extra Parmesan.
Rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 35 to 42 minutes. Cool completely.
Tip: Use the oil from the tomato jar in place of olive oil for intense flavor.

title: Everything Bagel Batter Bread

ingredients.prep: beaten with 1 tablespoon water, for egg wash

steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the warm water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Activate yeast in remaining warm water with sugar.
Add remaining flour (380g), salt, garlic powder, onion powder, olive oil, 1 egg, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 minutes.
Pour into greased pan. Brush top with egg wash (1 beaten egg + 1 tablespoon water). Coat generously with everything bagel seasoning.
Rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 35 to 42 minutes.
Cool completely. Slice and top with cream cheese.
Tip: Add 120g cubed cream cheese to the batter for cream cheese pockets inside.

title: Cinnamon Raisin Batter Bread
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the milk in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g milk come out of the recipe totals above, leaving 60g warm milk for the dough.)
Activate yeast in warm water and remaining milk (60g) with 10g of the sugar.
Add remaining flour (380g), remaining sugar (40g), salt, butter, egg, cinnamon, vanilla, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 minutes.
Fold in raisins.
Pour into greased pan. Sprinkle top with cinnamon sugar (20g sugar + 5g cinnamon).
Rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 35 to 42 minutes.
Cool in pan 10 minutes, then on rack.
Tip: The cinnamon sugar top caramelizes into a crispy, fragrant crust.

title: Lemon Blueberry Yeasted Batter Bread

ingredients.prep: fresh or frozen, tossed in 10g flour

steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the warm water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Activate yeast in remaining warm water (140g) and milk with 10g of the sugar.
Add remaining flour (380g), remaining sugar (50g), salt, butter, egg, lemon zest, lemon juice, vanilla, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 minutes.
Gently fold in blueberries.
Pour into greased pan. Rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 40 to 45 minutes.
Cool completely. Drizzle lemon glaze (100g powdered sugar, 30g fresh lemon juice).
Tip: Do not thaw frozen blueberries before folding in.

title: Chocolate Cherry Yeasted Batter Bread
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the warm water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Activate yeast in remaining warm water (180g) with 10g of the sugar.
Add remaining flour (350g), cocoa, remaining sugar (50g), salt, butter, egg, vanilla, espresso powder, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 minutes.
Fold in chocolate chips and cherries.
Pour into greased pan. Rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 40 to 45 minutes.
Cool completely.
Tip: Soak cherries in dark cherry juice or bourbon for extra intensity.

title: Maple and Walnut Yeasted Batter Bread
steps: Make tangzhong: combine 20g of the bread flour with 100g of the warm water in a microwave-safe bowl. Whisk smooth. Microwave in 30-second bursts, whisking each time, until a thick glossy paste forms. Cover surface with plastic wrap and cool to room temperature. (The 20g flour and 100g water come out of the recipe totals above.)
Activate yeast in remaining warm water (140g) and milk with 20g of the maple syrup.
Add remaining flour (380g), salt, remaining maple syrup (60g), butter, egg, vanilla, cinnamon, and cooled tangzhong. Beat 2 minutes.
Fold in toasted walnuts.
Pour into greased pan. Scatter a few extra walnuts on top.
Rise 1.5 to 2 hours. Bake at 190 C / 375 F for 38 to 45 minutes.
Cool completely. Drizzle maple glaze (100g powdered sugar, 30g pure maple syrup, 15g milk).
Tip: Toast walnuts in a dry pan 5 minutes before adding. Completely transforms the flavor.
Tip: Use Grade B (darker) maple syrup for the most intense maple flavor.

title: Lemon Ricotta Pancakes with Blueberry Compote

ingredients.prep: melted, plus more for the pan
fresh or frozen, divided, for the compote
for the compote, add a 4th if berries are tart

steps: Make the pancakes: Whisk all dry ingredients (flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, kosher salt) together in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, whisk egg yolks, ricotta, buttermilk, lemon zest, lemon juice, vanilla, and melted butter until smooth.
In a third clean bowl, beat egg whites with a hand mixer until stiff peaks form. Do not skip this step.
Pour wet mixture into dry and stir until just combined. Lumpy is fine.
Fold egg whites into the batter gently in two additions. Do not deflate them.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-low. Butter lightly.

Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form and edges look set, about 3 minutes. Flip and cook 2 more minutes. Keep heat at medium-low — these are richer than standard pancakes and burn easily.

Make the blueberry compote: Add 1 cup of the blueberries, sugar, lemon zest, lemon juice, water, and a pinch of salt to a heavy saucepan. Bring to a rolling boil, then immediately reduce to medium-low.
Simmer 7 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until berries have mostly burst and the mixture is mostly liquid.
Add the remaining 1 cup blueberries. Simmer another 6 to 7 minutes until deep violet and slightly thickened.
Serve the compote warm over the pancakes. The compote will thicken further as it cools.
notes: Tuning notes — More lemon flavor: add a 3rd lemon's zest or 1/4 tsp lemon extract. Sweeter pancakes: add 1 more tbsp of sugar to the batter. Thicker compote: stir in 1 tsp cornstarch dissolved in 1 tsp water in the last 2 minutes. Make-ahead: compote stores in the fridge up to 5 days; reheat gently on the stove or 30 seconds in the microwave. Makes ~10 pancakes plus 1.5 cups compote.

title: House-Made Sage Pork Sausage

ingredients.prep: 80/20 fat ratio, do not use lean

steps: Combine all seasonings in a bowl first, then add the cold ground pork.
Mix thoroughly with your hands until spices are evenly distributed throughout.
Form into 8 patties, roughly 1/2 inch thick. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before cooking.
Heat a skillet over medium-low. Cook patties 3 to 4 minutes per side until browned and cooked through.
notes: Key notes: Keep pork cold throughout mixing and forming — warm fat smears and makes patties greasy. Refrigerating before cooking helps patties hold their shape and improves texture. For best flavor, mix the night before and refrigerate overnight. Tuning notes — More sage-forward: bump sage to 2 tsp and omit fennel entirely on first batch. Slightly sweeter: add 1/2 tsp maple syrup to the mix. More heat: increase red pepper flakes to 1/4 tsp. Makes 8 patties.

title: Shakshuka

ingredients.prep: small handful, torn, add at end
crumbled, finish only, do not cook in

steps: Heat olive oil in a cast iron or heavy skillet over medium heat. Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute more. Add red pepper flakes and smoked paprika, stir and let bloom in the oil 30 seconds.

Pour in hand-crushed tomatoes with their juices. Add oregano, salt, and black pepper. Simmer uncovered 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until sauce thickens. Tear in fresh basil. Taste and adjust salt.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Use the back of a spoon to make wells in the sauce.
Crack each egg into a small bowl first, then gently lower into a well. Season eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Cover the skillet and cook until whites are just set but yolks are still runny, about 5 to 7 minutes.
Remove from heat. Crumble goat cheese over the top, scatter fresh cilantro, and serve immediately with toast.
notes: Based on the Paragary's menu (spiced tomato, goat cheese, cilantro, toast), this version skips bell pepper and heavy spice in favor of a clean, herb-forward tomato sauce. Goat cheese finishes the dish, not cooks into it. Key notes: Use hand-crushed San Marzano whole tomatoes — diced or pureed changes the texture. Cook the sauce down well before adding eggs; thin sauce leads to watery eggs. Goat cheese goes on at the very end, not while cooking. Tuning notes — More savory depth: add 1 tsp tomato paste with the garlic before the tomatoes. Slightly more complex: add 1/8 tsp cumin (bridges Italian and Middle Eastern without going full shakshuka). More heat: bump red pepper flakes to 1/2 tsp. Serve over polenta: skip the toast and ladle sauce and eggs over a bowl of cheesy polenta.

title: Creamy Cheesy Polenta
steps: Combine chicken stock and milk in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Once boiling, pour in polenta in a slow steady stream while whisking vigorously to prevent clumps.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Switch to a wooden spoon and stir frequently for 20 to 25 minutes until polenta is thick, pulls away from the sides, and tastes tender rather than gritty.
Remove from heat. Stir in butter until melted, then stir in both cheeses until fully incorporated.
Stir in heavy cream. Taste and adjust salt.
Serve immediately topped with extra Parmesan and fresh cracked black pepper.
notes: Key notes: Use stock instead of plain water for the liquid — it makes a significant difference in depth. Freshly grate both cheeses; pre-shredded bags do not melt cleanly. Stir frequently — the more you stir, the creamier the final texture. Add cheese off the heat so it melts in without clumping. Troubleshooting — Too thick before serving: stir in a splash of warm stock or milk to loosen. Leftovers: polenta firms up completely in the fridge; slice into cakes and pan-fry in butter for crispy polenta cakes the next morning. Pairing note: Serve the shakshuka directly over a bowl of this polenta instead of (or alongside) toast. The polenta absorbs the tomato sauce beautifully and makes for a more substantial brunch plate.

title: Vietnamese Vermicelli Bowl (Bun Bowl)

source.note: Bun Bowl — home version, inspired by Creasian Fusion

ingredients.prep: for the nuoc cham; Three Crabs or Red Boat recommended
for the nuoc cham; not bottled
for the nuoc cham; or to taste
for the nuoc cham; minced fine
for the nuoc cham; 1-2 chilies, or 1 tsp chili garlic sauce (Sambal)
for the nuoc cham; finely shredded
for the marinade; bottom 4 inches only, outer layers removed, minced very fine
for the marinade; avocado or vegetable
for the chicken; adds richness and helps browning
for the chicken; gives golden color
for the veggie build; 2-3 cups, roughly torn or chopped
for the veggie build; large, julienne or thin rounds
for the veggie build; blanch 30 sec for safety, or serve raw
for the veggie build; packed, non-negotiable
for the veggie build; regular basil works too
for the veggie build; medium, thin julienne strips
for the veggie build; 3-4 stalks, thin sliced
thin (bun) — not pho noodles, not glass noodles

steps: Make the nuoc cham first (it improves as it sits): Dissolve the sugar in the warm water. Add fish sauce, lime juice, and rice vinegar. Stir in the minced garlic and chili. Taste and adjust — it should hit sweet, sour, salty, and spicy in that order. Stir in the shredded carrot if using. Let sit 20+ minutes before serving; overnight is better. Don't skip the rice vinegar — it adds brightness that lime alone doesn't provide.

Make the marinade and marinate the chicken: Combine the universal lemongrass base (lemongrass, garlic, shallots, fish sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, neutral oil, black pepper) in a bowl, then stir in the coconut milk and turmeric for the chicken. Pound the chicken thighs to even thickness, then coat in the marinade. Marinate 4-8 hours or overnight.

Make the quick do chua: Toss the julienned daikon and carrot with the salt. Let sit 10 minutes, then rinse and squeeze dry. Mix the rice vinegar, sugar, and warm water until dissolved. Pack the vegetables in a jar and pour the brine over them. Ready in 1 hour, better after 24 hours.
Cook the chicken: Grill or sear the marinated thighs hot and fast until cooked through and nicely charred (the sugar and turmeric caramelize well on a hot grill). Let rest briefly, then slice.

Cook the noodles: Soak the rice vermicelli in cold water 20-30 minutes, then boil 1-2 minutes until just tender. Rinse under cold water immediately and drain well. Serve at room temperature — do not refrigerate or they will clump and stiffen.

Assemble in this order for texture and flavor layering: noodles as the base; lettuce layer on one side; fresh herbs (mint, basil) tucked in; cucumber, bean sprouts, cabbage, bell pepper, and tomato; do chua on top of the vegetables; sliced grilled chicken laid over everything; crispy shallots if you have them; drizzle nuoc cham over everything, or serve it on the side.
notes: Default protein here is chicken thighs (the most forgiving to cook — thighs over breasts, always). The same universal lemongrass base works for three other proteins, just swap the additions and marinating time: BEEF RIBEYE — add 1 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp soy sauce, and optionally 1/2 tsp five-spice powder; marinate 2-4 hours, slice thin against the grain, grill or sear hot and fast, brief rest before plating. PORK (shoulder, belly, or tenderloin) — add 1 tbsp honey (promotes caramelization) and 1/2 tsp turmeric (golden color); slice thin, marinate 2-4 hours minimum; the sugar and honey char beautifully on a hot grill. SHRIMP — use HALF the universal base quantity, then add zest of half a lime and a pinch of chili flake; marinate 20-30 minutes MAXIMUM (shrimp gets rubbery if it sits too long in acid or salt); skewer or use a grill basket. Nuoc cham tip: most restaurants water it down; this ratio keeps it punchy. Amounts in the veggie build are sized for 2 bowls.

title: Starbucks Lemon Loaf (Dupe)

ingredients.prep: for the loaf; from 1-2 large lemons
for the loaf; large, room temp
for the loaf; vegetable or canola

steps: Prep: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9x5" loaf pan and line with parchment paper.
Infuse: Add sugar and lemon zest to a large bowl. Rub together with your fingers until fragrant and faintly yellow. This releases the essential oils, the most critical step for strong lemon flavor.
Wet mix: Whisk in eggs until smooth. Add melted butter, oil, sour cream, milk, vanilla, lemon juice, and lemon extract. Whisk until pale and creamy.
Dry mix: Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a separate bowl.
Combine: Add dry ingredients to wet and fold gently with a spatula. Stop when just combined. Do not overmix.

Bake: Pour into pan. Run a spatula down the center lengthwise to create the signature Starbucks split. Bake 45-55 min until a toothpick comes out clean. If top browns too fast, tent loosely with foil in the last 10-15 min.
Cool: Cool in pan 10 min, then transfer to a wire rack. Cool completely before glazing (1-2 hours minimum).

Glaze: Sift powdered sugar into a bowl with a pinch of salt. Gradually add lemon juice, whisking until you reach a thick, almost-spreadable consistency. Add 1 tbsp milk for opacity. Do not over-thin. Pour over cooled loaf, spread to drip slightly over edges. Let set 30-60 min until firm.
notes: Lemon extract is the key ingredient for strong flavor — don't skip it or substitute with extra juice. Sour cream must be full-fat for the right texture. Do not glaze until the loaf is completely cool or the glaze will run off. Triple lemon delivery: zest in batter + lemon extract in batter + lemon juice in glaze. Tastes even better the next day. Store airtight at room temp up to 5 days; do not refrigerate (dries out); freeze unglazed up to 6 months. Pan: 9x5" loaf pan, 350°F, bake 45-55 min. Yield ~8 slices.

title: Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole

ingredients.prep: cut small, blanched 2 min and drained

steps: Make the Cheese Sauce: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in flour and cook 1 minute — it should smell slightly nutty. Slowly whisk in warm milk, then broth, adding each in a steady stream while whisking to prevent lumps. Cook, stirring, until thickened enough to coat a spoon, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in 1 cup of the cheddar, Dijon, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, pepper, and smoked paprika. Stir until smooth.

Combine the Filling: Preheat oven to 375F. In a large bowl, combine chicken, broccoli, and cooked rice. Pour the cheese sauce over and stir to coat everything evenly. Transfer to a greased 9x13 baking dish and spread level.
Add Topping: Scatter remaining 1/2 cup cheddar over the top. Toss panko with melted butter and smoked paprika, then spread over the cheese layer.
Bake: Bake uncovered at 375F for 30 to 35 minutes until the top is golden and the edges are bubbling. Let rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: Blanching the broccoli: Two minutes in boiling water, then drained and patted dry. This ensures the broccoli is tender in the final bake without releasing too much water into the sauce. Cheese: Shred your own cheddar from a block. Pre-shredded bags contain anti-caking agents that make the sauce grainy. Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. Reheat covered with foil at 325F for 20 minutes or microwave individual portions.

title: Classic Beef and Penne Casserole

ingredients.prep: cooked just under al dente and drained

steps: Brown the Beef: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high. Add onion and cook 4 minutes. Add beef and break it up. Cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat. Add garlic, Italian seasoning, oregano, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Cook 1 minute.
Add the Sauce: Add marinara and diced tomatoes. Stir to combine and simmer 5 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Combine with Pasta: Preheat oven to 375F. Add cooked penne to the beef sauce and stir to coat. If using a skillet that isn't oven-safe, transfer to a greased 9x13 baking dish. Spread level.
Top and Bake: Scatter mozzarella and parmesan over the top. Bake uncovered 25 to 30 minutes until cheese is melted, bubbly, and golden in spots. Rest 5 minutes. Top with fresh parsley before serving.
notes: Undercook the pasta: Pull it 2 minutes before the package says al dente. It finishes cooking in the oven and absorbs sauce. Fully cooked pasta turns mushy after baking. Drew option: Use hot Italian sausage in place of half the ground beef, or double the red pepper flakes. Ryan note: This is a Ryan-leaning recipe. The beef-forward, tomato-based profile is straightforward and reliable. Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. One of the best next-day reheats in the collection.

title: Chicken Mushroom Penne Bake

ingredients.prep: cooked just under al dente and drained

steps: Cook the Mushrooms and Aromatics: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add onion and cook 4 minutes. Add mushrooms and cook undisturbed 3 minutes until golden, then stir and cook 2 more minutes. Add garlic and thyme, cook 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside.

Make the Cream Sauce: In a saucepan over medium heat, melt butter and whisk in flour. Cook 1 minute. Slowly whisk in warm milk then broth. Stir until thickened, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in parmesan, garlic powder, nutmeg, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Combine: Preheat oven to 375F. In a large bowl, combine penne, chicken, and mushroom mixture. Pour cream sauce over and stir to coat. Transfer to a greased 9x13 baking dish and spread level.

Top and Bake: Toss panko with olive oil and spread over the casserole. Scatter parmesan over the panko. Bake uncovered 25 to 30 minutes until the top is golden and the edges bubble. Rest 5 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
notes: Don't crowd the mushrooms: Cook them in a single layer without stirring for the first 3 minutes. Crowding causes steaming instead of browning — you want golden edges, not gray mushrooms. Nutmeg: A small amount of nutmeg in cream sauces is a classic French technique. It adds warmth without tasting distinctly of nutmeg. Use a real nutmeg and a microplane if you have one. Drew option: Add a pinch of red pepper flakes to the cream sauce or finish Drew's portion with cracked black pepper.

title: Scalloped Potatoes with Ham

ingredients.prep: peeled and sliced 1/8 inch thin on a mandoline or sharp knife
diced into 1/2-inch pieces (deli ham or leftover baked ham)

steps: Make the Sauce: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in flour and cook 1 minute. Slowly whisk in warm milk and cream. Cook, stirring, until thickened enough to coat a spoon, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in 1/2 cup of the cheese, Dijon, salt, pepper, and nutmeg until smooth.

Layer the Casserole: Preheat oven to 375F. Grease a 9x13 baking dish. Layer one-third of the potato slices in overlapping rows. Scatter half the onion slices and half the ham over the potatoes. Pour one-third of the sauce over. Repeat layers, finishing with a final layer of potatoes and the remaining sauce poured over the top.
First Bake: Cover tightly with foil. Bake 40 minutes — the potatoes need this covered time to steam and become tender before the top browns.

Uncover and Finish: Remove foil. Scatter remaining 1/2 cup cheese over the top. Return to oven uncovered for 15 minutes until the top is golden and bubbling. Let rest 10 minutes before cutting — it sets as it rests.
notes: Potato thickness: Uniform thin slices are critical for even cooking. A mandoline set to 1/8 inch is the easiest way to get consistent results. Thicker slices will be undercooked in the center. Yukon Golds: They hold their shape better than Russets, which fall apart. They also have a natural buttery flavor that works well in cream sauces. Resting time: Don't skip the 10-minute rest after baking. The sauce tightens as it cools slightly and the casserole slices cleanly instead of falling apart. Ryan and Drew note: This one leans toward a maybe for both of you — but a good scalloped potato with real gruyere and ham is worth trying at least once.

title: Green Bean Casserole

ingredients.prep: adds depth without tasting like soy

steps: Blanch the Green Beans: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook green beans 4 minutes until just tender but still bright green. Drain and transfer immediately to an ice bath or run under cold water to stop cooking. Drain and pat dry.

Make the Mushroom Sauce: Melt butter in a large oven-safe skillet or saucepan over medium-high. Add onion and cook 4 minutes. Add mushrooms and cook undisturbed 3 minutes until golden. Add garlic and thyme, cook 1 minute. Sprinkle flour over and stir to coat. Cook 1 minute. Slowly whisk in broth then cream. Add soy sauce, salt, and pepper. Simmer, stirring, until sauce thickens, about 4 minutes.

Combine and Transfer: Preheat oven to 375F. Add blanched green beans to the mushroom sauce and stir to coat. Transfer to a greased 9x13 baking dish if not using an oven-safe skillet. Mix 1/2 cup of the fried onions into the top of the casserole.

Bake and Top: Bake uncovered 20 minutes. Remove from oven and scatter remaining fried onions and parmesan over the top. Return to oven for 10 more minutes until the topping is crisp and golden. Serve immediately — the topping softens as it sits.
notes: Why soy sauce: A small amount of soy sauce in mushroom-based sauces adds umami depth that rounds out the flavor. It doesn't taste like soy in the finished dish — it just tastes more savory. Topping timing: Add the final fried onion and parmesan layer in the last 10 minutes only. If they go on at the start they burn before the casserole is done. Drew note: Drew is the more enthusiastic audience for this one — but the scratch mushroom sauce version is significantly better than the canned soup original and may be the one to convert Ryan.

title: Sausage, Potato, and Pepper Bake

ingredients.prep: mild or sweet, cut into 1-inch rounds

steps: Season and Arrange: Preheat oven to 400F. On a large rimmed sheet pan or in a 9x13 baking dish, combine potatoes, peppers, onion, and garlic. Drizzle with olive oil and toss with Italian seasoning, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. Spread into a single layer.

Add the Sausage: Nestle the sausage rounds into and among the vegetables. Everything should be in roughly one layer — use two pans if your pan is too crowded. Crowding causes steaming instead of roasting.
Roast: Roast at 400F for 35 to 40 minutes, stirring once halfway through, until the potatoes are golden and fork-tender and the sausage is browned.
Finish: Remove from oven. Scatter parmesan over the top and return to the oven for 3 to 4 minutes until melted. Top with fresh parsley and serve directly from the pan.
notes: Single layer matters: If the vegetables are stacked, they steam in each other's moisture and won't brown. Use a large sheet pan or split between two pans. Drew option: Use hot Italian sausage or add extra red pepper flakes to Drew's portion before roasting. Variations: Add sliced zucchini or cherry tomatoes in the last 15 minutes of roasting. They don't need the full cook time. Storage: Keeps 4 days. Reheat in a 375F oven for 15 minutes to restore the crispiness — microwaving makes the potatoes soft.

title: Southwest Chicken and Black Bean Casserole

ingredients.item: Rotel diced tomatoes with green chiles
Mexican blend or Monterey Jack cheese

steps: Make the Sauce Base: Preheat oven to 375F. In a large bowl, stir together sour cream, softened cream cheese, salsa, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper until smooth. The cream cheese needs to be fully softened or it won't incorporate — microwave 30 seconds if needed.
Combine the Filling: Add chicken, black beans, corn, and drained Rotel to the sauce. Stir to coat everything evenly. Transfer to a greased 9x13 baking dish and spread level.
Add the Topping: Scatter shredded cheese evenly over the top. Sprinkle crushed tortilla chips over the cheese for crunch.
Bake: Bake uncovered at 375F for 25 to 30 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling and the edges are hot. Rest 5 minutes. Top with fresh parsley or scallions before serving.
notes: Cream cheese: It needs to be at room temperature to blend smoothly. Cold cream cheese leaves lumps in the sauce. Tortilla chip topping: Add the chips in the last 10 minutes if you want maximum crunch, or from the start if you don't mind them softening slightly into the cheese. Drew option: Use hot Rotel and hot salsa, or add sliced jalapenos to Drew's half of the casserole before baking. Storage: Keeps 4 days. The chip topping softens overnight — add fresh chips when reheating if crunch matters.

title: Cheesy Chicken and Rice Bake
steps: Mix the Casserole: Preheat oven to 350F. In a large bowl, whisk together cream of chicken soup, chicken broth, and sour cream until smooth. Add garlic powder, onion powder, salt, pepper, and smoked paprika. Stir in diced onion, frozen peas, and uncooked rice. Fold in the chicken.
Transfer and Top: Pour mixture into a greased 9x13 baking dish. Spread level. Scatter shredded cheddar over the top. Toss crushed Ritz crackers with melted butter and spread over the cheese.
Bake Covered: Cover tightly with foil and bake at 350F for 30 minutes. The rice needs covered steam time to cook through.
Uncover and Finish: Remove foil and bake uncovered 10 more minutes until the cracker topping is golden and the edges are bubbling. Rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: Uncooked rice: This recipe goes in with dry rice — it cooks in the soup and broth during the covered bake. Do not use pre-cooked rice here; it will turn mushy. Ritz cracker topping: Ritz crackers give a buttery, slightly salty crunch that works perfectly here. Panko is a fine substitute if you don't have them. Drew option: Add a diced jalapeno to the filling mix or use pepper jack in place of cheddar. Storage: Keeps 4 days. The rice absorbs liquid as it sits — add a splash of broth when reheating.

title: Italian Baked Penne

ingredients.prep: cooked just under al dente and drained

ingredients.item: fresh mozzarella or shredded low-moisture mozzarella

steps: Make the Meat Sauce: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook onion 4 minutes. Add beef and brown, breaking it up, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat. Add garlic, tomato paste, Italian seasoning, and basil. Cook 1 minute. Add red wine and let reduce by half, about 2 minutes. Add crushed tomatoes, salt, and pepper. Simmer 10 minutes.

Make the Bechamel: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium. Whisk in flour and cook 1 minute. Slowly whisk in warm milk. Stir until thickened, about 4 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in parmesan, nutmeg, salt, and white pepper.

Layer the Casserole: Preheat oven to 375F. Combine cooked penne with meat sauce and stir to coat. Pour into a greased 9x13 baking dish. Pour bechamel evenly over the top, spreading it gently to cover.
Top and Bake: Scatter mozzarella and parmesan over the bechamel layer. Bake uncovered 30 to 35 minutes until the top is golden and bubbling. Rest 10 minutes before serving.
notes: Two sauces: The meat sauce on the pasta and the bechamel on top is the classic Italian construction. It's more work than a single-sauce bake but the result is richer and more complex. Red wine: A dry red (Chianti, Merlot, or whatever you have open) deepens the meat sauce significantly. Beef broth works as a substitute but the flavor is less complex. Drew option: Add red pepper flakes to the meat sauce or use hot Italian sausage for half the meat.

title: Mexican Enchilada Casserole

ingredients.prep: finely diced, soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry

steps: Cook the Filling: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook onion 4 minutes. Add beef and break it up. Cook until browned, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat. Add garlic, chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and salt. Cook 1 minute. Stir in black beans and corn.

Layer the Casserole: Preheat oven to 375F. Spread 1/2 cup enchilada sauce on the bottom of a greased 9x13 dish. Lay a single layer of tortilla halves over the sauce, overlapping slightly. Spread half the beef filling over the tortillas. Pour 1/2 cup enchilada sauce over. Sprinkle 1/2 cup cheese. Repeat layers: tortillas, remaining filling, sauce. Finish with a final layer of tortillas, remaining sauce, and remaining cheese on top.
Bake: Cover tightly with foil and bake at 375F for 20 minutes. Remove foil and bake uncovered 10 more minutes until cheese is bubbly and the edges are hot.
Serve: Rest 5 minutes. Serve topped with sour cream, soaked red onion, and parsley.
notes: Corn vs flour tortillas: Corn tortillas hold up better in this casserole and give a more authentic flavor. Flour tortillas work but become very soft and can make the layers gummy. Enchilada sauce: Store-bought red enchilada sauce is fine here. Old El Paso and Las Palmas are reliable. Avoid the green (verde) sauce unless you want a different flavor profile. Drew option: Use hot enchilada sauce or add sliced jalapenos between the layers for Drew's half. Storage: Keeps 4 days. One of the best leftover casseroles in the collection — the flavors deepen overnight.

title: Greek Pastitsio-Style Beef and Pasta Bake

ingredients.prep: cooked just under al dente and drained

steps: Make the Meat Sauce: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook onion 4 minutes. Add beef and brown, breaking it up, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat. Add garlic, tomato paste, cinnamon, allspice, oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook 1 minute. Add wine or broth and let reduce by half. Add crushed tomatoes and simmer 10 minutes. The cinnamon is a background warm note, not a sweet flavor.

Make the Bechamel: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium. Whisk in flour and cook 1 minute. Slowly whisk in warm milk. Stir until thick, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool 5 minutes. Stir in eggs quickly, then 1/2 cup of the cheese, nutmeg, salt, and white pepper. The eggs must go in after the sauce has cooled slightly or they will scramble.

Assemble: Preheat oven to 375F. Combine cooked penne with meat sauce and spread in a greased 9x13 baking dish. Pour bechamel evenly over the top. Smooth with a spatula. Scatter remaining cheese over the bechamel.

Bake: Bake uncovered 35 to 40 minutes until the bechamel crust is golden and set — it should jiggle slightly but not be liquid when you shake the pan. Rest 15 minutes before cutting. It slices cleanly once it sets.
notes: The cinnamon: Cinnamon in the meat sauce is the defining flavor of pastitsio. It adds warmth, not sweetness. If it concerned you in the Moroccan stew, this is a good comparison — same principle, savory context. Eggs in the bechamel: The eggs set the bechamel into a firm crust during baking. Without them it stays soft and won't slice. Let the sauce cool to below 160F before adding them. Resting time: 15 minutes is not optional here. The bechamel crust needs time to firm up or it falls apart when cut.

title: French Chicken and Mushroom Gratin
steps: Cook the Mushrooms: Heat butter and olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high. Add onion and cook 4 minutes. Add mushrooms in a single layer and cook undisturbed 3 minutes until golden. Stir and cook 2 more minutes. Add garlic, tarragon, and thyme. Cook 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper.

Build the Sauce: Add white wine to the skillet and reduce by half, about 2 minutes. Add heavy cream, chicken broth, and Dijon mustard. Stir to combine. Simmer 4 to 5 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and coats the back of a spoon. Stir in nutmeg.

Add the Chicken: Preheat oven to 400F. Add shredded chicken to the skillet and stir to coat in the sauce. If your skillet is oven-safe, leave it in the skillet. Otherwise transfer to a greased baking dish.

Top and Bake: Scatter gruyere over the top. Toss panko with melted butter and parmesan and spread over the cheese. Bake uncovered at 400F for 20 to 25 minutes until the topping is deep golden and the sauce is bubbling at the edges. Top with fresh parsley.
notes: Tarragon: The defining herb of French chicken dishes. It has a delicate anise note that works beautifully with cream and mushrooms. Don't substitute with other herbs — use less or omit it entirely if you can't find it, rather than swap it for something else. Gruyere: Essential here — its nutty, slightly complex flavor is what makes this feel French rather than just a cream bake. Swiss cheese is a reasonable substitute. Drew option: Add a generous pinch of white pepper to the sauce and more cracked black pepper on top of Drew's portion.

title: Spanish Chorizo and Rice Bake

ingredients.prep: steeped in 2 tbsp warm water for 10 min

steps: Steep the Saffron and Brown the Chorizo: Steep saffron threads in 2 tbsp warm water — set aside. Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high. Brown chorizo rounds 2 minutes per side. Remove to a plate, leaving the rendered fat in the pan.

Build the Base: Preheat oven to 375F. In the same pan over medium heat, add onion and bell pepper. Cook 5 minutes. Add garlic, smoked paprika, and thyme. Cook 1 minute. Add uncooked rice and stir to coat in the fat, about 1 minute.

Add Liquids and Assemble: Add diced tomatoes, warm chicken broth, and the saffron with its steeping liquid. Stir to combine. Return chorizo to the pan. Nestle olives throughout. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer.

Bake: Cover tightly with foil or a lid and bake at 375F for 35 to 40 minutes until the rice has absorbed all the liquid and is fully cooked. Remove cover, scatter cheese over the top, and return to the oven uncovered for 5 minutes. Top with parsley and serve with lemon wedges.
notes: Saffron: A small amount goes a long way — it colors the rice golden and adds a subtle floral, slightly earthy flavor. It's expensive but a pinch is all you need. If unavailable, add an extra 1/2 tsp smoked paprika instead. Spanish chorizo: Use the cured, firm kind sold in a casing — not raw Mexican chorizo. The rendered fat from browning it is the flavor base for the whole dish. Manchego: An aged Spanish sheep's milk cheese that pairs naturally with chorizo. Parmesan is a fine substitute and easier to find.

title: Moroccan-Spiced Ground Beef and Chickpea Bake
steps: Make the Couscous Base: Place couscous in a bowl with olive oil and salt. Pour boiling broth over it, stir once, cover tightly, and let sit 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork. Spread into the bottom of a greased 9x13 baking dish.

Make the Beef and Chickpea Filling: Preheat oven to 375F. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook onion 4 minutes. Add beef and brown, breaking it up, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat. Add garlic and all spices. Cook 1 minute. Add crushed tomatoes and honey. Simmer 5 minutes. Stir in chickpeas.
Assemble: Spoon the beef and chickpea filling evenly over the couscous layer.

Top and Bake: Toss panko with olive oil, parsley, smoked paprika, and garlic powder. Scatter over the filling. Crumble feta over the panko. Bake uncovered at 375F for 20 to 25 minutes until the topping is golden and the edges are bubbling. Rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: The cinnamon: Same principle as the Moroccan chickpea stew — background warmth, not sweetness. It disappears into the blend of cumin, coriander, and paprika. Start with 1/4 tsp if you're cautious and increase next time. Couscous layer: The couscous soaks up the spiced juices from the beef layer as it bakes, making the bottom layer as flavorful as the top. Drew option: Extra cayenne in the filling, or serve with harissa stirred into Drew's portion. Storage: Keeps 4 days. The flavors deepen significantly on day 2.

title: Creamy Tuscan White Bean and Spinach Bake
steps: Cook the Aromatics and Greens: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook onion 4 minutes. Add garlic, Italian seasoning, and red pepper flakes. Cook 1 minute. Add spinach and stir until wilted, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat.

Make the Cream Sauce: In a saucepan over medium heat, melt butter and whisk in flour. Cook 1 minute. Slowly whisk in warm milk and broth. Stir until thickened, about 4 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in 1/2 cup parmesan, lemon juice, and nutmeg.
