Combine and Transfer: Preheat oven to 375F. In the skillet with the spinach mixture, add cannellini beans, sundried tomatoes, and olives. Pour the cream sauce over and stir gently to combine — the beans are delicate, fold rather than stir aggressively. Transfer to a greased 9x13 baking dish.

Top and Bake: Scatter mozzarella and remaining parmesan over the top. Toss panko with olive oil and spread over the cheese. Bake uncovered at 375F for 20 to 25 minutes until the top is golden and the sauce is bubbling at the edges. Rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: Cannellini beans: Rinse them well and fold gently — they fall apart if stirred aggressively. Intact beans give the casserole better texture than a mashed mass. Protein add-in: Stir in shredded rotisserie chicken with the beans if you want a heartier version. Sliced Italian sausage browned first also works well. Sundried tomatoes: Use the oil-packed kind, drained. They're more flavorful than the dry-packed variety and distribute better throughout the bake. Drew option: Double the red pepper flakes in the base or add a pinch of cayenne to the cream sauce. Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. Add a splash of broth when reheating — the sauce thickens considerably as it cools.

title: Garlic Butter Shrimp Scampi

ingredients.prep: peeled and deveined, tails on or off

steps: Cook the Pasta: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil. Cook pasta to al dente per package instructions. Before draining, reserve 1/2 cup pasta water. Drain and set aside.

Sear the Shrimp: Pat shrimp dry — moisture prevents a good sear. Heat 1 tbsp butter and the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add shrimp in a single layer. Cook 90 seconds per side until pink and just curled. Remove to a plate.

Build the Sauce: In the same pan over medium heat, add remaining 3 tbsp butter. Add garlic and cook 60 seconds until fragrant but not browned. Add white wine and let it reduce by half, about 2 minutes. Add lemon juice, zest, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper.

Finish and Combine: Add the drained pasta to the pan and toss to coat, adding splashes of reserved pasta water to loosen as needed. Return shrimp to the pan. Toss everything together and cook 30 seconds. Remove from heat and fold in parsley.
notes: Pasta water: The starch in reserved pasta water emulsifies the butter-wine sauce and helps it cling to the noodles. Don't skip saving it. Drew option: Double the red pepper flakes or finish Drew's bowl with a pinch of cayenne. No wine: Substitute chicken broth plus an extra tbsp of lemon juice. The sauce will be a little less complex but still good.

title: Korean Beef Bulgogi Bowls

ingredients.prep: very thinly sliced against the grain

steps: Marinate the Beef: Combine all marinade ingredients (soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, pear, black pepper) in a bowl. Add the sliced beef and toss to coat. Let sit while you prep everything else — even 10 minutes makes a difference. The pear enzymes start tenderizing immediately.
Quick-Pickle the Cucumber: Toss cucumber slices with rice vinegar, sugar, salt, and sesame oil. Set aside. They'll be ready by the time the beef is cooked.

Cook the Beef: Heat a large skillet or wok over high heat until very hot. Cook beef in batches — don't crowd the pan or it will steam instead of sear. Each batch takes 2 to 3 minutes. High heat and quick cooking is the key.
Build the Bowls: Divide rice into bowls. Top with beef, drained pickled cucumber, scallions, and sesame seeds.
notes: Slicing the beef: Freeze the beef for 20 to 30 minutes before slicing — it firms up and makes thin slicing much easier. Against the grain is essential for tenderness. Drew option: Add gochujang (1 tbsp) to the marinade or serve with a side of gochujang sauce. Storage: Cooked bulgogi keeps 3 days. The marinade doubles well if you want to prep extra for the week.

title: Shakshuka
steps: Build the Sauce Base: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet (cast iron is ideal) over medium-high. Add onion and bell pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 6 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.

Add Spices and Tomatoes: Add cumin, smoked paprika, coriander, and cayenne. Stir and cook 30 seconds until fragrant. Pour in the crushed tomatoes, add salt, pepper, and sugar. Stir to combine and reduce heat to medium. Simmer 8 to 10 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.

Add the Eggs: Use a spoon to create 6 shallow wells in the sauce. Crack one egg into each well. Cover the pan with a lid and cook over medium-low heat until whites are set but yolks are still runny, about 5 to 7 minutes. Watch carefully — overcooked yolks are hard to reverse.
Finish and Serve: Scatter feta and parsley over the top. Serve directly from the pan with plenty of crusty bread or warm pita for scooping.
notes: Yolk doneness: For firmer yolks, cook 2 to 3 minutes longer with the lid on. The whites should be completely opaque with no jiggle before you call them done. Drew option: Add an extra 1/4 tsp cayenne to the sauce, or serve with harissa on the side. Additions: A handful of baby spinach stirred into the sauce before adding eggs works well. So does a dollop of Greek yogurt served alongside.

title: Miso Butter Pasta
steps: Cook the Pasta: Cook pasta in heavily salted boiling water to al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.

Make the Miso Butter Base: In a large skillet over medium heat, melt butter. Add garlic and cook 60 seconds. Whisk in the miso paste until fully incorporated into the butter — it will look grainy at first, keep whisking. Add wine or sake and let reduce by half, about 2 minutes.
Build the Sauce: Add soy sauce, lemon juice, and pepper. Stir to combine. Add 1/3 cup of the reserved pasta water and whisk until smooth and glossy.

Finish the Pasta: Add drained pasta to the skillet and toss to coat, adding more pasta water a splash at a time until the sauce clings and looks silky. Remove from heat. Add parmesan and toss again. Top with scallions and sesame seeds.
notes: Miso paste: White (shiro) miso is mild and slightly sweet. Red miso will work but is saltier and more intense — reduce soy sauce by half if substituting. Drew option: A drizzle of chili crisp or a pinch of red pepper flakes over Drew's bowl adds heat that works perfectly with the miso. Add protein: This sauce is excellent with seared scallops, shrimp, or sliced chicken thighs added in step 4.

title: French Onion Pasta

ingredients.item: dry white wine or dry sherry

steps: Cook the Onions Fast: Heat butter and olive oil in a large wide skillet over medium-high heat. Add onions, salt, and sugar. Cook, stirring every 2 to 3 minutes, for 15 minutes total. Turn the heat up if they're just sweating — you want real color. Scrape up any browned bits from the pan. They won't be as deep as slow-caramelized onions but they'll have good flavor and color.
Cook the Pasta: While the onions cook, boil pasta to al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water before draining.

Build the Sauce: Add garlic and thyme to the onions and cook 1 minute. Add wine or sherry and cook until nearly evaporated, about 2 minutes. Add beef broth and Worcestershire. Simmer 3 minutes until slightly reduced.
Combine and Finish: Add pasta to the skillet and toss to coat. Splash in pasta water as needed. Remove from heat and fold in the shredded cheese until melted and glossy. Top with parsley.
notes: The onions: Fifteen minutes on medium-high gets you about 70% of the way to fully caramelized. It won't be identical to the 45-minute version, but the wine, broth, and Worcestershire cover the rest of the flavor gap. Cheese: Gruyere melts beautifully and is the classic French onion choice. Swiss works well. Avoid pre-shredded bags — they have anti-caking agents that prevent smooth melting.

title: Thai Basil Beef
steps: Mix the Sauce: Combine oyster sauce, soy sauce, fish sauce, brown sugar, and water in a small bowl. Stir until sugar dissolves. Set aside.
Cook the Aromatics: Heat oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until shimmering. Add garlic and shallots and stir-fry 30 seconds. Add bell pepper and cook 1 minute.

Brown the Beef: Add ground beef and break it up with a spatula. Cook over high heat, pressing into the pan to get browning on the meat rather than just steaming it. Cook until no pink remains, about 4 to 5 minutes.

Sauce and Finish: Pour the sauce over the beef and toss to coat. Cook 1 minute until sauce is absorbed and everything is glossy. Remove from heat and fold in the basil leaves — they'll wilt in the residual heat. Serve over jasmine rice, topped with a fried egg if using.
notes: Thai basil vs Italian basil: Thai basil has an anise note that's more authentic here. Italian basil works fine as a substitute — the dish is still excellent. Do not use dried basil. Fried egg: A runny fried egg on top is traditional and makes the dish richer. Fry it in a separate pan while the beef finishes. Drew option: Add 1 to 2 Thai chilies or a spoonful of chili crisp to Drew's portion. This dish is traditionally quite spicy.

title: Chicken Piccata
steps: Dredge the Chicken: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge both sides in flour, shaking off the excess. The thin flour coating is what creates the slightly crisp exterior and helps the sauce cling.
Sear the Chicken: Heat olive oil and 1 tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove to a plate.

Build the Pan Sauce: In the same pan over medium heat, add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Add white wine and scrape up any browned bits. Let reduce by half, about 2 minutes. Add chicken broth and reduce another 2 minutes. Add lemon juice, zest, and capers.

Finish with Butter: Remove from heat and swirl in the remaining 2 tbsp cold butter. This emulsifies the sauce and gives it a glossy, restaurant-quality finish. Return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over the top. Garnish with parsley.
notes: Thin-cut chicken: Thin-cut breasts or pounded breasts cook fast and evenly. Thick breasts need longer in the pan and the flour coating will over-brown before the inside is done. Butter finish: Add the butter off the heat. If the pan is too hot when you add butter, it breaks the sauce and turns greasy. Serving: Excellent over angel hair pasta, mashed potatoes, or with crusty bread to soak up the sauce.

title: Moroccan Spiced Chickpea and Tomato Stew
steps: Start the Couscous: Place couscous in a large bowl with oil and salt. Pour boiling water or broth over it, stir once, and cover tightly with a plate or plastic wrap. Let sit 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork. It's done and can wait while you finish the stew.

Build the Base: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or Dutch oven over medium-high. Add onion and cook 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and all spices and cook, stirring, 60 seconds until fragrant. The cinnamon is subtle here — it adds warmth, not sweetness.
Add Tomatoes and Chickpeas: Add crushed tomatoes, chickpeas, and broth. Stir to combine. Add salt and honey. Bring to a simmer and cook 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the stew thickens.
Finish and Serve: Stir in lemon juice. Taste and adjust salt. Serve over couscous, topped with fresh parsley.
notes: The cinnamon: It's a background note, not a featured flavor. If it concerns you, start with half and taste after the stew simmers. It rounds out the spice blend rather than making the dish taste sweet. Drew option: Extra cayenne in the pot, or serve with harissa on the side. Add protein: A handful of shredded rotisserie chicken stirred in at the end works well if you want it heartier. Storage: This stew improves the next day. Keeps 4 days refrigerated.

title: Beef and Lamb Kofte with Yogurt Sauce

ingredients.prep: grated on a box grater, excess liquid squeezed out
thinly sliced and soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry

steps: Make the Yogurt Sauce: Combine Greek yogurt, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, herbs, and salt. Stir and set aside. It gets better as it sits.

Mix the Kofte: Combine all kofte ingredients in a bowl and mix with your hands until just combined. Don't overwork — overmixing makes the texture dense. Form into oval patties about 3 inches long and 3/4 inch thick. You should get 12 to 14 patties.
Cook the Kofte: Heat a large skillet or grill pan over medium-high. Cook kofte 3 to 4 minutes per side until browned and cooked through. Work in batches if needed — don't crowd the pan.
Build the Pitas: Spread yogurt sauce on warm pita. Add 3 kofte per pita, cucumber slices, soaked red onion, and a squeeze of lemon.
notes: Grating the onion: Grating instead of dicing distributes the onion flavor throughout the meat without leaving visible chunks. Squeezing out the liquid is important — too much moisture and the patties won't hold. Beef-only version: Skip the lamb and use 1.5 lbs ground beef. The flavor is less complex but still excellent. Drew option: Add 1/4 tsp cayenne to the kofte mix or serve with a side of harissa.

title: Soy Glazed Salmon with Bok Choy
steps: Make the Glaze: Whisk together soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and cornstarch slurry in a small saucepan. Heat over medium, stirring, until it thickens enough to coat a spoon, about 3 minutes. Set aside.

Sear the Salmon: Pat salmon dry. Season with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Place salmon skin-side up. Sear 3 to 4 minutes without moving until a golden crust forms. Flip and cook 2 to 3 minutes more depending on thickness.
Glaze and Finish Salmon: Spoon glaze over the salmon in the last minute of cooking. It will caramelize quickly — watch it closely. Remove salmon to a plate.

Cook the Bok Choy: In the same pan over medium-high, add oil. Place bok choy cut-side down and cook 2 minutes until lightly charred. Flip and add soy sauce. Cook 1 minute more until wilted. Serve salmon and bok choy over rice, topped with sesame seeds and scallions.
notes: Salmon doneness: For medium, the center should still be slightly translucent when you check with a fork. It continues cooking off the heat. Overcooked salmon is dry — err on the side of less. Drew option: Add a squirt of sriracha or a pinch of red pepper flakes to the glaze.

title: Spanish Chorizo and White Bean Skillet

ingredients.prep: cured, not raw Mexican chorizo, sliced into rounds

ingredients.item: dry sherry or dry white wine

steps: Brown the Chorizo: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add chorizo rounds and cook 2 to 3 minutes per side until lightly browned and the fat has rendered into the pan. Remove chorizo to a plate. Leave the rendered fat in the pan — that's flavor.
Cook the Aromatics: In the same pan over medium heat, add onion to the rendered chorizo fat. Cook 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic, smoked paprika, and thyme and cook 1 minute.
Deglaze and Build: Add sherry or wine and scrape up any browned bits. Cook until nearly evaporated, about 2 minutes. Add diced tomatoes and chicken broth. Stir to combine.

Add Beans and Finish: Add the white beans and return the chorizo to the pan. Simmer 8 to 10 minutes until the sauce reduces and thickens slightly around the beans. Taste and adjust salt. Top with parsley. Serve with crusty bread.
notes: Spanish vs Mexican chorizo: This recipe uses Spanish chorizo — the hard, cured kind sold in a casing, like a firm sausage. Mexican chorizo is raw and crumbled and is a completely different ingredient. Check the label. Sherry: Dry sherry (Fino or Manzanilla) is the most authentic choice here. Dry white wine is a fine substitute. Storage: Keeps 4 days. The beans absorb the sauce as it sits and become even more flavorful.

title: Vietnamese-Style Lettuce Wraps with Ground Pork
steps: Mix the Sauce: Combine fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, lime juice, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil. Stir until sugar dissolves. Set aside.

Brown the Pork: Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add ground pork and break it up. Cook, pressing into the pan for browning, until cooked through and lightly crisped in places, about 6 to 7 minutes.
Add the Sauce: Pour the sauce over the pork and stir to coat. Cook 1 to 2 minutes until the liquid reduces and coats the meat. Taste and adjust lime or fish sauce.
Build the Wraps: Spoon pork into lettuce leaves. Top with shredded carrots, cucumber, scallions, mint, basil, and sesame seeds. Squeeze lime over the top.
notes: Fish sauce: It smells strong from the bottle but cooks into a deep savory flavor. Don't skip it — it's doing most of the work in this dish. No cilantro: Traditional versions use cilantro heavily. Mint and basil give you the fresh herb component without it. Drew option: Add sliced Thai chilies to Drew's wraps or serve with a small dish of sambal oelek on the side.

title: Italian Sausage and Gnocchi Skillet
steps: Brown the Sausage: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add sausage and break it into chunks. Cook until browned and cooked through, about 6 minutes. Remove to a plate, leaving fat in the pan.

Crisp the Gnocchi: Add gnocchi to the pan in a single layer. Cook undisturbed over medium-high for 2 to 3 minutes until the bottoms are golden and crisp. Flip and cook 2 more minutes. This is the step most people skip — don't skip it.
Build the Sauce: Add garlic to the pan and cook 30 seconds. Add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, red pepper flakes, and Italian seasoning. Stir to combine and simmer 3 minutes.
Finish: Return sausage to the pan. Add spinach and stir until wilted, about 1 minute. Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Top with grated parmesan.
notes: Gnocchi: Shelf-stable gnocchi (from the pasta aisle) works better for pan-frying than refrigerated. Refrigerated gnocchi has more moisture and steams rather than crisps. Drew option: Use hot Italian sausage, or add extra red pepper flakes to Drew's bowl.

title: Honey Garlic Chicken Thighs
steps: Season the Chicken: Pat chicken thighs completely dry — this is the key to crispy skin. Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika on both sides.

Sear Skin-Side Down: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high. Place chicken skin-side down. Cook undisturbed for 8 to 10 minutes until the skin is deeply golden and releases easily from the pan. Flip and cook 3 minutes on the other side. Remove to a plate.

Make the Sauce: Pour off excess fat, leaving about 1 tbsp in the pan. Over medium heat, add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Add honey, soy sauce, vinegar, and broth. Stir and bring to a simmer. Cook 2 to 3 minutes until slightly reduced.

Finish in Sauce: Return chicken skin-side up to the pan. Spoon sauce over the chicken and cook over medium heat 5 to 7 minutes, basting occasionally, until chicken is cooked through (internal temp 165F). Swirl in butter to finish the sauce.
notes: Dry skin: Moisture is the enemy of crispy skin. Pat thoroughly and don't rush the sear. If the skin sticks, it's not ready to flip — give it another minute. Drew option: Add red pepper flakes or a squirt of sriracha to the sauce. Serving: Excellent with steamed rice, roasted vegetables, or mashed potatoes.

title: Creamy Tuscan White Bean and Sausage Soup
steps: Brown the Sausage: Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high. Add sausage and cook, breaking into chunks, until browned, about 5 minutes. Remove to a plate.
Cook the Aromatics: In the same pot, add onion and cook 4 minutes. Add garlic, Italian seasoning, smoked paprika, and red pepper flakes. Cook 1 minute.
Build the Soup: Add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, and beans. Return sausage to the pot. Bring to a simmer and cook 10 minutes.
Cream, Greens, and Finish: Stir in heavy cream. Add kale or spinach and stir until wilted, about 2 minutes. Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Ladle into bowls and top with grated parmesan.
notes: Thickening: For a thicker soup, mash about 1/2 cup of the beans against the side of the pot before adding the cream. The starch thickens the broth. Kale vs spinach: Kale holds up better and adds texture. Spinach wilts completely and gives a smoother result. Either works. Drew option: Use hot Italian sausage or add more red pepper flakes.

title: Greek Lemon Chicken Soup
steps: Cook the Rice in Broth: Bring chicken broth to a boil in a large pot. Add rice and cook until just tender, about 15 minutes.
Make the Egg-Lemon Mixture: While the rice cooks, whisk egg yolks and lemon juice together in a bowl until smooth and slightly frothy.

Temper the Eggs: This is the key step — if you add cold egg yolks directly to hot soup, they scramble. Slowly ladle 1 cup of hot broth into the egg-lemon mixture while whisking constantly. This brings the eggs up to temperature gradually.

Finish the Soup: Reduce heat to low. Pour the tempered egg mixture back into the pot slowly while stirring. Add shredded chicken. Do not boil after this point — it will break the texture. Heat gently 2 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon zest. Serve topped with fresh dill.
notes: Tempering: Slow and steady. Add the hot broth to the eggs one ladle at a time while whisking. Rushing this step gives you lemon scrambled eggs in soup — still edible, but not what you're going for. Broth quality: This soup has very few ingredients so the broth flavor matters. Use a good store-bought broth or homemade. Low-sodium gives you more control over seasoning. Storage: The rice continues to absorb liquid as it sits. The soup will thicken considerably overnight — thin it with extra broth when reheating.

title: Spicy Peanut Noodles

ingredients.item: lo mein noodles, ramen noodles, or spaghetti

ingredients.prep: tossed in after draining to prevent sticking

steps: Cook the Noodles: Cook noodles per package instructions. Drain, rinse with cold water, and toss immediately with sesame oil to prevent clumping.

Make the Peanut Sauce: Whisk together all sauce ingredients, adding warm water 1 tbsp at a time until the sauce is pourable but still thick. Taste — it should be savory, slightly sweet, and have a mild background heat. Adjust soy, vinegar, or chili sauce to taste.
Toss and Serve: Pour peanut sauce over noodles and toss until every strand is coated. Divide into bowls. Top with cucumber, scallions, peanuts, and sesame seeds. Serve with lime wedges.
notes: Natural peanut butter: Use a natural peanut butter (just peanuts and salt). Conventional peanut butters with added sugar and oil give the sauce a sweeter, less savory profile. Drew option: Double the chili garlic sauce in Drew's bowl or add a drizzle of chili crisp on top. Add protein: Shredded rotisserie chicken or sliced pan-seared tofu added on top turns this into a more complete meal. Serving temperature: This works at room temperature or cold. The sauce thickens as it chills — thin with a splash of water or soy sauce if serving from the fridge.

title: Mediterranean Stuffed Peppers

ingredients.prep: any color, tops cut off and seeds removed

ingredients.item: reserved tomato liquid from the can

steps: Make the Filling: In a large skillet over medium-high, cook ground beef and onion together, breaking up the beef, until browned, about 5 minutes. Add garlic, cumin, oregano, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook 1 minute. Add drained diced tomatoes and cooked rice. Stir to combine. Remove from heat and fold in feta and parsley.

Fill the Peppers: Pack the filling firmly into each pepper. They should be well-stuffed. Place peppers upright in a large deep skillet or pot that fits them snugly — they need to support each other or they'll tip.

Stovetop Steam: Pour chicken broth and reserved tomato liquid around (not over) the peppers. Bring to a simmer over medium heat. Cover tightly and cook 20 to 25 minutes until the peppers are tender when pierced with a knife.
Finish and Serve: Remove lid and top each pepper with additional crumbled feta. Spoon the pan liquid over the peppers as a sauce.
notes: Stovetop vs oven: Covering the skillet and cooking in liquid steams the peppers from the outside while the filling finishes from the inside. No oven required and the texture is nearly identical. Pepper selection: Red, yellow, and orange peppers are sweeter. Green peppers are more bitter and slightly firmer. Any combination works — use what's on sale. Drew option: Add a pinch of cayenne to the filling.

title: Provencal Pan-Seared Chicken with Olives and Tomatoes
steps: Season and Sear the Chicken: Season chicken thighs with salt, pepper, and herbes de Provence. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken 4 to 5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through. Remove to a plate.
Build the Sauce: In the same pan over medium heat, add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Add cherry tomatoes and cook 2 minutes until they start to burst and soften. Add olives and capers.
Deglaze and Reduce: Add wine and scrape up any browned bits from the pan. Cook until wine reduces by half, about 2 minutes. Add chicken broth and thyme. Simmer 3 minutes until the sauce has body.
Finish and Serve: Return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over the top. Swirl in butter. Sprinkle with fresh parsley. Serve with crusty bread, rice, or orzo.
notes: Herbes de Provence: A dried herb blend sold in most grocery stores — typically thyme, rosemary, oregano, and lavender. If unavailable, substitute an equal amount of Italian seasoning plus a pinch of dried rosemary. Chicken thighs: Boneless thighs are far more forgiving than breasts — they stay moist even if slightly overcooked and take on sauce flavor better. Serving: This is excellent with crusty French bread to soak up the sauce, or over orzo pasta.

title: Beef Taco Skillet

ingredients.item: shredded Mexican blend or Monterey Jack cheese

ingredients.prep: finely diced and soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry

steps: Brown the Beef: In a large skillet over medium-high, cook ground beef, breaking it up, until browned, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat if needed, leaving about 1 tbsp.
Add Seasoning and Vegetables: Add all taco seasoning to the beef and stir to coat. Cook 1 minute. Add black beans, corn, and diced tomatoes with chiles. Stir to combine.
Simmer: Add broth and stir. Bring to a simmer and cook 8 to 10 minutes until the liquid reduces and the mixture thickens.

Add Cheese and Serve: Scatter shredded cheese over the top and cover with a lid for 2 minutes until melted. Serve directly from the skillet. Top with sour cream and soaked red onion. Squeeze lime over the top. Serve with tortilla chips or in flour tortillas.
notes: Rotel: Diced tomatoes with green chiles. Find it in the canned tomato aisle. Original is mild. Hot adds noticeable heat — a simple way to dial up the spice level. Drew option: Use Hot Rotel, or add a chopped jalapeno when browning the beef. Serving: Scooped with tortilla chips it works as a dip. Spooned into flour tortillas it works as tacos. Over rice it becomes a burrito bowl. Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. One of the better meal prep options in the collection.

title: Greek Style Chicken Salad

ingredients.prep: white meat only, pulled or chopped

steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, lemon juice, red wine vinegar, fresh garlic, garlic powder, oregano, salt, and pepper until smooth. The dressing should taste bold and slightly over-seasoned at this stage. It evens out once the chicken goes in. The red wine vinegar replicates the tang that was built into the TJ's mayo.

Soak the Onion (optional but worth it): Finely dice the red onion and soak in cold water for 5 minutes, then pat dry. This removes the harsh raw bite while keeping the flavor. Skip if you prefer the sharpness.

Prep the Mix-Ins: Drain and roughly chop the artichoke hearts. Halve the Kalamata olives. Drain the oil from the sundried tomatoes and chop into small pieces. Smaller pieces of sundried tomato distribute the flavor better and prevent any one bite from being overwhelming.

Combine: Add the chicken, red onion, artichoke hearts, olives, and sundried tomatoes to the dressing bowl. Fold in the dill and parsley. Mix gently until everything is coated. Fold in the feta last if using. Feta is salty, so taste before adding any extra salt.

Rest Before Serving: Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. The yogurt dressing needs time to bind and the herbs need to bloom into the mix. An hour is better. Taste again after resting and adjust lemon or salt if needed.
notes: Homemade improvement on the Trader Joe's original. Rest 30 min minimum. Greek oregano: the most distinctly Mediterranean herb here and the biggest upgrade over the TJ's version. Look for dried Greek or Mediterranean oregano; it's more aromatic and slightly bitter compared to Italian oregano. Costco chicken: Pull only the breast meat for the cleanest texture. Let it cool completely before mixing; warm chicken breaks down the yogurt dressing. The bird is already well-salted, so hold back on extra salt until after the rest period. If it seems dry: a small drizzle of the oil from the sundried tomatoes jar stirred into the dressing fixes it without changing the flavor. Storage: Keeps 3 to 4 days refrigerated. The dressing thickens as it sits; a splash of lemon juice loosens it back up.

title: Smoky Chipotle Chicken Salad

ingredients.prep: white meat only, pulled or chopped
minced, range 1-2 tsp, start with 1 and taste
frozen roasted corn, thawed, or char fresh corn in a dry pan
finely diced and soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry

steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, lime juice, chipotle in adobo, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Start with 1 tsp chipotle — you can always add more after tasting. The dressing should taste bright, smoky, and slightly sharp before the chicken goes in.
Soak the Onion: Finely dice the red onion and soak in cold water for 5 minutes, then pat dry. This removes the harsh raw bite while keeping the flavor.
Prep the Mix-Ins: If using fresh corn, char it in a dry cast iron or skillet over high heat for 3 to 4 minutes until you see some dark spots. Let it cool. Rinse and drain the black beans well.
Combine: Add the chicken, corn, black beans, soaked onion, and celery to the dressing bowl. Fold in the parsley. Mix gently until everything is coated. Taste and adjust lime or chipotle as needed.
Rest Before Serving: Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. The cumin and chipotle flavors deepen significantly as it sits.
notes: Southwest-inspired with smoky heat, roasted corn, and a lime-cumin yogurt dressing. Rest 30 min minimum. Chipotle in adobo: One or two chipotles from a can plus a small spoonful of the sauce; the sauce carries as much flavor as the pepper itself. Freeze the rest of the can in a zip bag flat and break off portions as needed. Drew option: Stir an extra teaspoon of the adobo sauce into Drew's portion, or add a few dashes of hot sauce at the table. Serving: Works especially well in a warm flour tortilla or stuffed in a pita; also excellent on thick-cut crackers. Storage: Keeps 3 to 4 days refrigerated. The corn softens slightly but the flavor improves.

title: Lemon Herb Chicken Salad with Artichoke

ingredients.prep: white meat only, pulled or chopped
finely diced and soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry

steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the yogurt, mayonnaise, Dijon, lemon juice, lemon zest, garlic, salt, and pepper. The dressing should taste very bright and tangy. The Dijon adds a mild heat that rounds out as it rests.
Soak the Onion: Finely dice the red onion and soak in cold water for 5 minutes, then pat dry.
Prep the Mix-Ins: Drain the artichoke hearts and capers well. Roughly chop the artichokes into bite-sized pieces. Leave the capers whole — they burst when you bite them.
Combine: Add chicken, artichoke, capers, celery, and onion to the dressing bowl. Fold in the tarragon and parsley. Mix gently. Taste and adjust lemon or salt.
Rest Before Serving: Refrigerate at least 30 minutes. The tarragon in particular needs time to soften into the dressing.
notes: A lighter, brighter companion to the Greek salad — capers, tarragon, and a sharp Dijon-lemon dressing. Rest 30 min minimum. Tarragon: Fresh tarragon has a mild anise note that complements the lemon and artichoke. If it's not available, fresh dill works well as a substitute. Dried tarragon is acceptable but use half the amount. Capers: Rinse them briefly if you find them too salty; they add a concentrated briny punch that works with the lemon. Storage: Keeps 3 to 4 days refrigerated. Add a small squeeze of lemon if it tastes flat after sitting.

title: Sesame Ginger Chicken Salad

ingredients.prep: white meat only, shredded into thin pieces

steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the mayonnaise, sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, ginger, garlic, honey, and pepper. The dressing will look thin — it thickens once the chicken absorbs it. Taste: it should be savory, slightly sweet, and have a clean sesame finish.
Shred the Chicken: Pull the chicken into thin shreds rather than chunks. This recipe works better with a finer texture so the dressing coats everything evenly.
Prep the Mix-Ins: Thinly shred the cabbage. Drain and roughly chop the water chestnuts. Slice the scallions.
Combine: Add the chicken, cabbage, water chestnuts, and scallions to the dressing. Toss gently until fully coated. Fold in the sesame seeds last.
Rest and Serve: Refrigerate at least 20 minutes. The cabbage will soften slightly and the ginger flavor will bloom. Taste and adjust soy or rice vinegar before serving.
notes: Toasted sesame, soy, and fresh ginger with shredded chicken — clean and satisfying with a light creamy base. Rest 20 min minimum. Sesame oil: Use toasted sesame oil, not plain. It's darker and carries the full sesame flavor; a little goes a long way. Drew option: Add a spoonful of chili crisp to Drew's portion and stir it in. It layers heat and additional umami without changing the base recipe. Serving: Excellent in lettuce wraps or on rice crackers; also works well in a flour tortilla with a little extra sesame oil drizzled in. Storage: Best within 2 days. The cabbage softens noticeably by day 3 but the flavor is still good.

title: Classic Deli Chicken Salad, Elevated

ingredients.prep: white meat only, pulled or chopped
finely diced and soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry
chopped, use if you have it

steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the mayonnaise, Greek yogurt, Dijon, lemon juice, red wine vinegar, dried dill, salt, pepper, and onion powder. Taste it — it should be tangy and dill-forward. The yogurt lightens the mayo base without losing richness.
Soak the Onion: Finely dice the red onion and soak in cold water for 5 minutes, then pat dry.
Dice Everything Fine: The celery and pickles should be diced small and even so every bite has a little of everything. Avoid large chunks here — this recipe is about consistent texture throughout.
Combine: Add the chicken, celery, pickles, and onion to the dressing. Fold in the fresh dill if using. Mix gently. Taste and adjust salt or lemon.
Rest Before Serving: Refrigerate at least 30 minutes. This one particularly benefits from a full hour — the pickles and dill integrate into the dressing and the flavor rounds out.
notes: The sandwich standard, done right — clean, creamy, dill-forward, with the crunch of celery and pickles. Rest 30 min minimum. Pickles: Use dill pickles, not bread and butter. The sweetness of bread and butter pickles throws off the balance. The pickle brine can also be stirred in (1 tsp) if the dressing needs more tang. Fresh vs. dried dill: Dried dill goes in the dressing where it hydrates and blooms. Fresh dill is folded in at the end for brightness. If you only have one, dried in the dressing is more important. Storage: Keeps 4 days refrigerated. This is the most stable of the collection — the flavors actually improve on day 2.

title: Ryan's Tuna Salad, Measured and Refined

ingredients.item: Boar's Night Out White Lightning seasoning

steps: Drain the Tuna Well: Press the tuna firmly in the can with the lid to remove as much water as possible before opening fully. Dump into a bowl and break up any large chunks with a fork. Better drainage means the dressing sticks instead of pooling at the bottom.

Mix the Dressing: Stir together the mayonnaise, red wine vinegar, dill, White Lightning, and pepper. The vinegar adds a brightness that balances the mayo. Taste before adding to the tuna — White Lightning varies in saltiness by batch.
Add the Mix-Ins: Add the celery, onion, black olives, and pickles to the tuna. Pour the dressing over and fold gently. Avoid over-mixing — you want defined pieces, not a paste.
Rest Before Serving: Cover and refrigerate at least 20 minutes. The dill and pickles bloom into the mayo and the whole thing comes together. Taste after resting and adjust seasoning.
notes: The recipe you already make, formalized — same flavors, real measurements, a few small technique upgrades. Serves 3-4. Rest 20 min minimum. White Lightning: This seasoning is doing a lot of work. Start at 1/2 tsp and taste after resting — it's easy to add more, impossible to take it out. If you're out, a pinch of garlic powder, onion powder, and a little celery salt gets close. Onion: Regular white or yellow onion works here instead of red. If you prefer less bite, soak it the same way: cold water 5 minutes, pat dry. Storage: Keeps 3 days refrigerated. Stir before serving — the mayo settles.

title: Mediterranean Tuna Salad

ingredients.prep: finely diced and soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry

steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the Greek yogurt, lemon juice, lemon zest, olive oil, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper. This is a no-mayo dressing — it will be lighter and brighter than most tuna salads. It should taste assertively lemony before the tuna goes in.
Soak the Onion: Finely dice the red onion and soak in cold water for 5 minutes, then pat dry.
Prep the Mix-Ins: Drain the artichoke hearts and capers well. Roughly chop the artichokes. Finely chop the sundried tomatoes — smaller pieces distribute the concentrated flavor better.
Combine: Add the tuna to the dressing and fold gently. Add the olives, capers, artichoke, sundried tomatoes, and onion. Fold in the parsley. Taste and adjust lemon or salt.
Rest Before Serving: Refrigerate at least 20 minutes. The yogurt dressing needs time to bind to the tuna.
notes: Bold, briny, and herb-forward — the tuna counterpart to the Greek chicken salad, with a yogurt-lemon dressing instead of mayo. Serves 3-4. Rest 20 min minimum. No mayo: This recipe intentionally skips mayo in favor of Greek yogurt and olive oil. The result is lighter and works better with the bright Mediterranean flavors. If you want more richness, add 1 tbsp mayonnaise to the dressing. Greek oregano: Use Greek or Mediterranean oregano, not Italian. The flavor is more intense and slightly bitter in a way that works well here. Storage: Keeps 3 days refrigerated. The lemon flavor stays bright longer than mayo-based salads.

title: Smoky Tuna Salad with Roasted Red Pepper

ingredients.prep: jarred, drained and chopped into bite-sized pieces
finely diced and soaked 5 min in cold water, patted dry

steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the mayonnaise, Greek yogurt, lemon juice, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. The smoked paprika should be immediately prominent. Taste — it should be smoky and savory with a slight tang from the lemon.
Soak the Onion: Finely dice the red onion and soak in cold water for 5 minutes, then pat dry.
Prep the Mix-Ins: Drain the roasted red peppers well and pat them dry — excess liquid will thin the dressing. Chop into roughly 1/2-inch pieces.
Combine: Add the tuna to the dressing and fold gently. Add the roasted red peppers, celery, olives, and onion. Fold in the parsley. Taste and adjust paprika or lemon.
Rest Before Serving: Refrigerate at least 20 minutes. The smoked paprika deepens as it sits.
notes: Deep smoked paprika flavor, roasted red peppers, and a creamy mayo-yogurt base — rich and bold without any actual fire. Serves 3-4. Rest 20 min minimum. Smoked paprika: Use smoked (not sweet or hot) Spanish paprika. It's what gives this recipe its depth; standard paprika will not produce the same result. Drew option: Add a pinch of cayenne (1/8 to 1/4 tsp) to the dressing for Drew's portion. It adds heat that complements the smokiness without changing the base flavor. Roasted red peppers: Jarred peppers in water work better than oil-packed here — less to drain and the flavor is cleaner. Pat them thoroughly dry before chopping. Storage: Keeps 3 days refrigerated. The paprika flavor intensifies on day 2.

title: Sesame Soy Tuna Salad
steps: Build the Dressing: Whisk together the mayonnaise, sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, ginger, garlic, honey, and pepper. The dressing will be thin and assertively flavored — this is intentional. Tuna absorbs it quickly. Taste: it should be salty, savory, slightly sweet, and have a clean sesame note.
Prep the Mix-Ins: Dice the cucumber small and remove the seeds with a spoon first — the seeds carry too much water. Slice the scallions thin.
Combine: Add the tuna to the dressing and fold gently. Add the cucumber and scallions. Fold in the sesame seeds last. Taste and adjust soy sauce or rice vinegar.
Rest and Serve: Refrigerate at least 15 minutes. This one moves quickly — the flavors are bright and don't need a long rest. Taste again before serving.
notes: Light, umami-forward, and wrap-ready — soy, toasted sesame, rice vinegar, and ginger with a minimal mayo base. Serves 3-4. Rest 15 min minimum. Sesame oil: Use toasted sesame oil — the dark kind. It carries all the sesame flavor; plain sesame oil has very little. Drew option: Sriracha stirred into Drew's portion works perfectly here; so does a drizzle of chili crisp over the top. Cucumber: Seed it and dice it small. Large cucumber pieces make the salad watery by day 2. If making ahead, add cucumber right before serving. Serving: Excellent in lettuce wraps or on rice crackers. The lighter dressing makes it ideal for lighter serving formats. Storage: Best within 2 days. Add fresh cucumber if serving on day 2.

title: Mexican Street Corn Chicken Salad

ingredients.prep: from ½ lime, for the sauce

steps: Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized cubes, then add to a bowl with the lime juice, avocado oil, chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well and marinate 15 to 30 minutes.
Heat a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken and cook 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until browned and cooked through to 165°F.

Drain the canned corn well and pat dry with paper towel. In a clean hot pan, add the corn in a single layer and let it sit untouched 2 to 3 minutes, then stir and cook another 2 to 3 minutes until charred in spots.
In a bowl, whisk the Greek yogurt, lime juice, hot sauce, Tajín, water, salt, and black pepper until smooth.
In a large bowl, combine the charred corn, red onion, red bell pepper, cilantro, jalapeños, and cotija cheese.
Pour in the sauce and mix until everything is coated.
Divide the chicken and the street corn salad into 4 servings. Store chilled and eat within 3 to 4 days.
notes: Meal-prep recipe. Per serving (4 servings): ~475 cal, 58g protein, 30g carbs, 18g fat.

title: Bacon, Egg & Cheese Rice Paper Spiral
steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Scramble 4 of the eggs over medium-low heat until just set but still soft, then set aside.
Crack the remaining egg into a shallow bowl and whisk until smooth.
Dip each of the 3 rice paper sheets into the beaten egg, coating both sides.
Lay the coated sheets on a lightly oiled surface, slightly overlapping to form one large sheet.
Add the scrambled eggs down the center, then top with the chopped bacon and shredded mozzarella.
Roll tightly into a cylinder, sealing as you go, then gently coil into a spiral.
Brush the top with any remaining egg and sprinkle generously with everything bagel seasoning.
Bake at 400°F for 15 to 20 minutes, until golden and crisp.
notes: Full recipe also at thechefoutwest.com.

title: Mango Lassi Chia Pudding with Cardamom Date Crumble
steps: Make the mango milk: in a blender, combine the cubed mango, plant-based yogurt, coconut milk, soy milk, vanilla, kosher salt, ground cardamom, and maple syrup. Blend until smooth.
Pour the mango milk into a jar or bowl and stir in the chia seeds until evenly distributed. Stir again after about 5 minutes to keep them from clumping.
Cover and refrigerate overnight (at least 4 hours), until thick and gelled.
Make the crumble: pulse the walnuts, coconut flakes, medjool dates, cardamom, and a pinch of salt in a food processor until it forms a sticky, textured crumble.
To serve, top the chia pudding with the cardamom date crumble. Adjust sweetness with extra maple syrup to taste.
notes: About 10g fiber per serving. Full recipe with swaps on the creator's site.

title: Banana Pudding Overnight Oats
steps: Blend the rolled oats and chia seeds together until finely ground.
Mix the ground oats with the Fairlife milk, 1 tbsp vanilla yogurt, and vanilla to make the oat base.
Make the pudding mixture: stir together the ½ cup vanilla yogurt, sugar-free Cool Whip, and sugar-free vanilla pudding mix until smooth.
In a jar, layer half the oat base, then half the pudding mixture, then a few crushed Nilla wafers and banana slices. Repeat with the remaining oat base, pudding mixture, wafers, and banana.
Cover and refrigerate overnight. For best results, add the banana on top in the morning so it doesn't brown.
notes: Per serving: 573 cal, 64g carbs, 16g fat, 44g protein.

title: Salted Maple Banana Bread
steps: Cream the softened butter and both sugars together.
Add the mashed banana and mix until combined.
Add the eggs, vanilla paste, and molasses, then mix again. The butter will split — don't worry.
In a separate bowl, whisk the flour, baking soda, and salt.
Combine the wet and dry ingredients.
Add the buttermilk half at a time, mixing thoroughly after each addition.
Pour into a lightly sprayed, parchment-lined loaf pan.
Bake at 325°F until deeply golden and firm to the touch (200–205°F internal temperature).
Out of the oven, immediately pour the maple syrup on top — it absorbs fast, so brush it on in small sections with a pastry brush.
Top with flaky sea salt and let cool completely before slicing.
notes: Even better the next day. At its peak if you slice it, wrap each slice in plastic, freeze, then thaw — the moisture redistributes and takes it to the next level.

title: Peaches & Cream Dirty Soda

ingredients.prep: diced, skin on, for the syrup
large, zested and juiced, for the syrup

steps: Make the syrup: in a small bowl, massage the sugar and lemon zest together with your fingers until fragrant and evenly incorporated.

In a small saucepan over medium-low heat, combine the diced peaches, the sugar-zest mixture, the lemon juice, and the water. Simmer 15–20 minutes until the peaches soften and break down, mashing them a bit with a wooden spoon as they cook. Stir in the vanilla bean paste.
Strain the syrup to remove the solids, pressing to extract as much liquid as possible. Save the leftover peaches — they're basically jam.
Chill the syrup completely in the refrigerator.
Make a soda: to an ice-filled glass add 2–4 tbsp of peach syrup (to taste), then top with sparkling water.

For a dirty soda: blend ½ a peach with about ¼ cup water and strain to make peach juice. Add 2 tbsp syrup to an ice-filled glass, top with ¼–⅓ cup peach juice, then sparkling water and about 1 tbsp heavy cream (or coffee creamer). Stir and enjoy.
notes: Syrup keeps in the fridge and makes several sodas. Save the strained peaches as a quick jam.

title: Cozy Chicken Cream Stew
steps: Prep all the vegetables first.
Heat a drizzle of olive oil in a pot and brown the chicken thoroughly, then remove it.
Add the mushrooms and onion with a pinch of salt and sauté until soft. Add the garlic and cook 2 minutes more.
Deglaze the pot with the sake. Once it no longer smells like alcohol and has reduced to almost no liquid, add the flour and mix well.
Add the chicken stock, the browned chicken, and the herb bundle. Simmer uncovered for 20 minutes.
Remove the herb bundle. Add the heavy cream, potatoes, and carrots and cook until tender.
If it's not thick enough, mix a couple tablespoons of flour with ⅓ cup water, stir it in, and simmer 5 more minutes.
Stir in the spinach to wilt, and season with salt and pepper. Serve with toasty sourdough, with a drizzle of truffle oil on top if you like.
notes: Inspired by the cream stew in Witch Hat Atelier. A Japanese-style white cream stew — cozy comfort food.

title: French Eggs Baked on Toast in Cream
steps: Warm the cream with the garlic, rosemary, and nutmeg over low heat for 5 minutes, until fragrant.
Fry the sourdough in the butter with a drizzle of olive oil, 2–3 minutes each side, until golden.
Crack the eggs over the toast, strain the warm cream on top, season, and scatter over the grated cheese.
Bake at 180°C fan (about 350°F) for 8 minutes, until the whites are just set and the cream is gently bubbling.
Finish with chopped chives and serve immediately.
notes: An old-school French classic (Oeufs au Plat Bressane). Ready in about 20 minutes.

title: One-Pot Sausage and Fennel Rigatoni

source.note: @heresyourbite on TikTok (one-pot spin on Ina Garten's)

ingredients.prep: freshly grated, plus more for serving

steps: Brown the sausage in a large pot, breaking it into pieces, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté the onion and fennel until softened. Add the crushed fennel seeds, garlic, and red pepper flakes.
Add the tomato paste and let it caramelize, then stir it through.
Pour in the white wine, then add the chicken broth and the uncooked rigatoni.
Simmer, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is tender and has soaked up the liquid.
Finish with a splash of cream and simmer to thicken. Stir in the Dijon, the cooked sausage, Parmesan, lemon zest, and butter if using.
Stir in the fresh parsley, taste for salt and pepper, and serve with extra Parmesan.
notes: A one-pot spin on Ina Garten's sausage and fennel rigatoni — fewer dishes to wash.

title: Sweet and Sticky Chicken
steps: Slice the chicken thighs into 1-inch pieces. In a bowl, combine with the dark soy, light soy, rice vinegar, oyster sauce, grated garlic, brown sugar, honey, and sesame oil. Marinate at least 15 minutes (or overnight).
Preheat a pan over medium-high heat. Add a bit of oil and the chicken with all its marinade.
Cook to an internal temperature of at least 165°F — the sauce will thicken as it cooks.
Serve over rice, topped with green onions if you like.
notes: Episode 11 of 'recipes every home cook should know.'

title: Lemon Caesar Yogurt Marinated Chicken

ingredients.prep: 1½–2 lb, ~4 breasts sliced in half and pounded thin

steps: Slice each chicken breast in half horizontally into thinner cutlets. Pound between plastic wrap to an even ~¼-inch thickness. Season both sides with salt and pepper.

In a large baking dish, whisk together the Greek yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, minced garlic, yellow mustard, anchovy paste, Worcestershire, grated Parmigiano-Reggiano, and black pepper until smooth.
Add the cutlets and turn to coat evenly. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour (no more than 24).
Let the chicken come to room temperature for about 20 minutes before cooking.

Pan-fry: heat 2–3 tbsp olive oil in a large pan over medium-high. Add the chicken and cook 2–3 minutes per side — try not to flip for the first couple minutes so it doesn't stick. Remove at 160°F internal, cover with foil, and rest 5 minutes (it keeps rising).
Finish with extra parmesan, black pepper, and a squeeze of lemon.
notes: Air fryer: 375°F, ~7 min per side to 165°F. Oven: 425°F on parchment, 15–20 min to 165°F. Marinate no longer than 24 hours.

title: Spicy Chili Shrimp Rice Paper Wontons

ingredients.prep: whole, medium, thinly sliced, for the sauce

steps: Chop the shrimp into fine chunks and thinly dice the green onions.
In a bowl, combine the shrimp, green onions, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, cornstarch, and white pepper and mix well to make the filling.
In a small bowl, mix the chili crisp, rice vinegar, soy sauce, minced garlic, brown sugar, sesame oil, sesame seeds, and thinly sliced green onions to make the sauce.
Wet a sheet of rice paper in water and fold it in on all sides to form a square roughly 2.5 inches across.
Place 1–2 tbsp of filling in the center and pinch all the corners together tightly, completely sealing the filling inside to form a rice-paper wonton.
Place the wontons on a sheet of non-stick baking paper, set in a steamer, and steam 8–10 minutes until the shrimp is cooked.
Transfer the wontons to a bowl, pour the sauce over the top, and enjoy.
notes: Per serving: 34g protein, 68g carbs, 11g fat, 516 cal. Scale the ingredients up to make more servings.

title: Blueberry Muffin Tops
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F / 190°C and line a sheet pan with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy, 2–3 minutes. Beat in the egg and vanilla until fully incorporated.
Add the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt and mix until combined. Pour in the milk and stir with a spatula until a very thick batter forms.
In a small bowl, toss the blueberries with about a tablespoon of flour, then fold the coated blueberries into the batter.
Refrigerate the batter at least 30 minutes.
Make the streusel: rub the butter into the flour and sugar with a fork or your fingers.
Scoop the batter with a 3-tablespoon cookie scoop (don't flatten it). Roll the top and sides of each ball in streusel, leaving the bottom bare, and place on the sheet pan ~3 inches apart.
Bake 12–15 minutes, until the edges begin to brown and the tops turn golden. Cool completely on a wire rack before enjoying.
notes: Makes 10. Chilling the batter keeps them from spreading too much.

title: Patatas Bravas
steps: Peel, wash, and cube 5 lb of gold potatoes.
Bring a pot of water to a boil with a heavy pour of salt and 2 tsp baking soda. Par-boil the potatoes 8–10 minutes, then lay them out flat to cool completely.
Fry in 350°F oil for about 5 minutes. Double-fry until golden brown, rest on a cooling rack, and salt immediately after the second fry.

Make the bravas sauce: in a pan over medium heat, add ⅓ cup olive oil, the minced garlic, and the flour. Cook about 1 minute, then add the smoked paprika, cayenne (if using), chicken stock, and sherry vinegar, and season with chicken bouillon or salt. Whisk frequently and cook ~8 minutes until thickened.
Make the garlic aioli: mix the mayo, juice of ½ lemon, olive oil, minced garlic, and salt.
Serve the potatoes fresh from the fryer, topped with the (lukewarm) bravas sauce and a drizzle of garlic aioli.

title: Crispy Honey Chicken

ingredients.prep: or cooking sake, for the marinade

steps: Cut the chicken into 1cm pieces. Add the salt, chicken powder, baking soda, ½ tbsp cornstarch, and mirin (or cooking sake). Mix well and marinate 10 minutes.
For the batter, first mix the plain flour, cornstarch, and oil. Add the water and stir until smooth, then add the baking powder and mix again. It should be thin enough to lightly coat your finger.
Add the chicken to the batter and mix until every piece is coated.
Heat oil to 180°C. Add the chicken pieces one by one. Don't move them for at least 1 minute or the batter may fall off. Fry about 3 minutes until golden.
Raise the oil to 190–200°C and fry again for about 15 seconds. Remove and drain.

Pour out the oil. Add the honey, sugar, and rice vinegar to the wok and cook over medium-high heat until the sugar melts and the syrup bubbles. Add the fried chicken and toss quickly until coated — as it cools slightly the glaze forms a thin crunchy honey shell.
For restaurant-style plating, fry a handful of vermicelli noodles until puffed, set the honey chicken on top, and finish with toasted sesame seeds.

title: Blueberry Cheesecake Chia Pudding
steps: Add the blueberries, almond milk, protein powder, and pudding mix to a blender and blend until smooth.
Add the chia seeds and blend again on high until it starts to thicken to a pudding-like texture.
Pour into jars, add the toppings, and store in the fridge for up to 4 days.

title: Honey Roasted Carrots
steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
Place the whole carrots in a baking dish and drizzle with olive oil; mix until completely coated.
Pour on the honey, season with salt and pepper, and mix until evenly coated.
Bake until just tender, or cooked to your liking, 40 minutes to 1 hour.

title: Irish Heritage Cabbage
steps: Preheat your oven's broiler.
Place the cabbage in a large pot, add the water, and bring to a boil. Simmer over low heat until tender, about 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the bacon in a skillet over medium-high heat until crisp; drain and set aside.
Drain the cabbage and drizzle with the melted butter. Sprinkle with the bacon and nutmeg and transfer to a baking dish.
Broil until the top is lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Serve with salt, pepper, and red wine vinegar to taste.

title: Chicken Kebabs with Turmeric and Chile

source.note: Mark Bittman, NYT Cooking (chef Edward Khechemyan, Adana LA)

steps: Combine all the ingredients in a nonreactive dish (or a resealable bag) and marinate in the refrigerator between 3 hours and overnight.
Discard the onions (or grill them separately if you like) and thread the chicken onto skewers. Heat a charcoal or gas grill; the flame should be fairly low and the rack about 6 inches from the heat.
Grill the chicken slowly, turning as necessary and basting with any leftover marinade, until browned all over, 15 to 20 minutes.
notes: Marinate at least 3 hours. The chef grills slowly over a low flame, not too close to the fire, until gorgeously browned.

title: Strawberry Soda

ingredients.prep: hulled and rinsed (or frozen), for the syrup

steps: Make the strawberry syrup (blender method): purée the strawberries with 1 cup water. Pour into a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, stir in the sugar, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium and stir occasionally until reduced by half, about 15 minutes (lower the heat if it foams up). Cool and store airtight in the fridge up to 1 week (yields about 2 cups).
Make the soda: combine ½ cup strawberry syrup, the club soda, and the lime juice in a measuring cup and stir well.
Divide between two ice-filled glasses, add sliced strawberries, and serve immediately.
notes: No blender: simmer the strawberries, sugar, and 1 cup water ~10 min, mash thoroughly, reduce 5 more min, then strain. Adjust to taste — more syrup for sweeter, more lime/soda for lighter. Club soda or seltzer is fizzier than sparkling water.

title: Crispy Garlic Smashed Potatoes
steps: Boil the whole potatoes in a large pot of water for 20 minutes, or until tender. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Grease a baking sheet with non-stick spray. Place the potatoes on it and gently smash each with a fork or potato masher — keep them together, just flattened. Bake for 20 minutes.
Whisk together the minced garlic and melted butter, pour over the potatoes, and season with salt and pepper. Bake another 5–7 minutes.

title: Hunan Chicken Salad

ingredients.prep: rotisserie is best, cubed or shredded

steps: Cook the Chinese noodles per the package. Rinse with cold water to stop the cooking and cool them, then place in a large bowl.
Cube or shred the chicken and add to the noodles.
Grate the cucumbers (de-seed and peel first if using standard cucumbers) and add to the chicken and noodles.
In a separate bowl, whisk all the dressing ingredients until combined — gently melting the peanut butter helps it mix in. Adjust vinegar, garlic, ginger, chili oil, and cayenne to taste.
Toss the dressing with the chicken, noodle, and cucumber mixture.
notes: Serve with a fruit salad to cut the heat if needed.

title: Skillet Breakfast Hash
steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a medium bowl, season the hash browns with the salt, pepper, and paprika; toss to combine.
In a skillet, cook the bacon until crispy, set aside, and remove the excess fat.
Heat the oil in the skillet, then spread the hash browns to take the shape of the pan and form a nest. Cook about 7 minutes over medium so the bottom forms a crispy crust.
In a bowl, whisk the eggs, cream, chopped bacon, and cheddar. Pour over the hash browns and spread evenly.
Bake 20 minutes, until golden brown and the eggs are cooked through.
Sprinkle with chives, slice, and serve hot — great with ketchup or hot sauce.

title: Ham and Egg Breakfast Braid
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Whisk the 8 eggs, heavy cream, salt, pepper, onion powder, and garlic powder to combine.
Heat a nonstick pan over medium and scramble the egg mixture until soft and almost fully cooked.
Flatten the puff pastry and cut it into thirds, then in half. On one piece, cut 3 strips with a paring knife, leaving about 1 inch at the end to hold it together, and braid the strips gently.
On a second piece, place ham, scrambled egg, and cheese, leaving room around the edges. Lay the braid over the top and brush with egg wash.
Place the braids on a lightly greased baking sheet and bake 25–30 minutes, until risen and deep golden brown. Serve warm.
notes: Makes about 6 braids.

title: Simple Lentil Soup

source.note: 'Struggle Meals, Lentils and Love' by Zee (She_Writes_Black)

ingredients.prep: brown or green; red or yellow for a creamier soup
1 packet, or your choice of seasonings

steps: Cook the lentils using a ratio of 1 cup lentils to 3 cups water (about 7 cups water for a whole bag — lentils double in size). Add a garlic clove and bay leaf for flavor.
Bring to a rolling boil, add the onion soup mix (or your seasonings), cover, and simmer on low 20–30 minutes to your desired tenderness.
For a heartier soup, add vegetables like potatoes, celery, onions, corn, and carrots. Adjust water and seasoning for any extra ingredients.
Thicken if you like by mashing some of the lentils or stirring in a slurry. Serve over rice or with cornbread.
notes: Budget meal-kit recipe. Optional add-ins: chicken, sausage, or beef. Use broth instead of water for more flavor. Add a little parsley to finish.

title: Lentil Sloppy Joes

source.note: 'Struggle Meals, Lentils and Love' by Zee (She_Writes_Black)

steps: 1 cup dry lentils replaces 1 pound of ground meat. Cook using 1 cup lentils to 2½ cups water, with a garlic clove, half an onion, and a bay leaf for flavor.
Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer 15–20 minutes until just tender. Drain excess liquid and let the lentils dry slightly for better sauce absorption.
Mix the cooked lentils with sautéed vegetables (if using) and the sloppy joe sauce. Season with salt, pepper, and herbs, then simmer to thicken.
Serve on hamburger buns with your favorite toppings, or over rice.
notes: Homemade sauce: combine 1 (15 oz) can tomato sauce, 1 tbsp Dijon or yellow mustard, 1 tbsp Worcestershire, brown sugar, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper to taste. 1 cup lentils makes about 4 servings. Optional: add ground beef, sausage, chicken, or turkey.

title: Lentil Bolognese

source.note: 'Struggle Meals, Lentils and Love' by Zee (She_Writes_Black)

ingredients.prep: brown or green for texture, red for a creamier sauce, dry

steps: 1 cup dry lentils replaces 1 pound of ground meat. Cook using 1 cup lentils to 2½ cups water (use 2 cups water for red lentils), with a garlic clove, half an onion, and a bay leaf.
Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer 15–20 minutes until just tender (12–15 minutes for red lentils). Drain excess liquid, let dry slightly, and mash ever so slightly.
Mix the lentils with sautéed vegetables (if using) and the spaghetti sauce. Season with salt, pepper, and herbs, then simmer to thicken.
Serve over your favorite pasta, topped with cheese and your usual pasta toppings.
notes: Great to make in batches and freeze. Optional: add ground beef, sausage, chicken, or turkey.

title: Lentil Shepherd's Pie

source.note: 'Struggle Meals, Lentils and Love' by Zee (She_Writes_Black)

steps: 1 cup dry lentils replaces 1 pound of ground meat. Cook using 1 cup lentils to 2½ cups water with a garlic clove, half an onion, and a bay leaf.
Bring to a boil with the onion soup mix and any seasonings, then cover and simmer 15–20 minutes until just tender (not mushy).
If using fresh or frozen vegetables, sauté until softened; if using canned, just drain (don't cook). Prepare the instant mashed potatoes per the package. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Combine the lentils (don't drain all the liquid) with the vegetables, season to taste (onion powder, garlic powder, rosemary), and simmer about 3 minutes to thicken.
Pour into a baking dish, spread the mashed potatoes evenly on top, add cheese if desired, and bake 15–20 minutes until golden brown.
notes: Serve by itself or with dinner rolls, biscuits, or bread. Optional: add ground beef, sausage, chicken, or turkey.

title: Lentil Chili

source.note: 'Struggle Meals, Lentils and Love' by Zee (She_Writes_Black)

steps: If using fresh vegetables, sauté diced bell peppers and onions in a large pot until translucent.
Add the lentils, water, tomatoes, dried minced onion (if not using fresh), corn, tomato paste, and chili seasoning. Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer 20–30 minutes until the lentils are tender.
Remove about 2 cups of the chili, blend or mash it, and return it to the pot to thicken. Cook a few more minutes until thick.
Serve over rice or with cornbread, plus your favorite chili toppings.
notes: Homemade chili seasoning: combine paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, chili powder, bay leaves, salt, and pepper to taste. Optional: add ground beef, sausage, chicken, or turkey, and toppings like cheese, sour cream, corn chips, green onions.

title: Lentil Tacos

source.note: 'Struggle Meals, Lentils and Love' by Zee (She_Writes_Black)

ingredients.prep: hard or soft (or a taco dinner kit)

steps: 1 cup dry lentils replaces 1 pound of ground meat. Cook using 1 cup lentils to 2½ cups water (2 cups for red lentils), with a garlic clove, half an onion, a bay leaf, and an ancho chili for flavor.
Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer 15–20 minutes until just tender. Drain excess liquid and let dry slightly.
Prepare the Spanish rice per the package.

Mix the cooked lentils with sautéed vegetables (if using), then add taco seasoning and a little water until combined — the mixture should be juicy but not saturated. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve in your favorite shells with toppings; serve the rice on the side or in the taco.
notes: Optional toppings: onions, bell peppers, lettuce, tomatoes, cheese. Optional protein: ground beef, sausage, chicken, or turkey.

title: Layered Dip

ingredients.prep: 16 oz, your favorite, for the bean layer
shredded, or more to taste, for the bean layer
16 oz jar (Arriba! Fire Roasted Mexican Red, medium), drained — reserve the liquid for the meat
or homemade: cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, salt, pepper
finely shredded, 1–2 packages (8 oz each), enough to fully cover

steps: Bean layer: Heat the can of refried beans in a saucepan on the stove. As it warms, stir in the can of roasted green chilis. Once hot, stir in about a cup of sharp cheddar (more to taste). When fully melted and incorporated, set aside to cool completely.
Guacamole: Use your favorite — homemade or store-bought, chunky is great. You’ll want about 2 cups.
Sour cream layer: Mix the sour cream with a full packet of taco seasoning (or to taste) until fully combined. Set aside.
Salsa: Put the fire-roasted salsa in a fine-mesh strainer to drain off most of the liquid — keep the drained liquid for the meat.

Meat layer: Brown the ground beef, stirring vigorously to break it into a very fine crumble (Taco Bell texture). When mostly cooked, add the Mexican seasoning and the reserved salsa liquid. Cook until most of the liquid evaporates and the meat is fine-grained and flavorful — moist but not wet, so the dip doesn’t get soupy.

Assemble: Spread the cooled bean mixture on a platter or in a large casserole dish. Spread the guacamole over it (it goes under the sour cream so it won’t brown). Dollop the sour cream mixture all over the guacamole and gently spread to fully seal it. Sprinkle the drained salsa over the sour cream. Scatter the meat in an even layer — don’t push or mix the layers; sprinkle as evenly as you can, then gently spread. Fully cover with the shredded Mexican-blend cheese.

Garnish generously with the diced tomatoes, sliced olives, sliced green onion, and pickled jalapeños (roughly a cup of each, to taste) — pile on plenty so every chip gets toppings. Serve with tortilla chips.
notes: Make it vegetarian by omitting the meat layer and using vegetarian refried beans (not "original," which has lard). For less heat, use tamed/mild jalapeños or omit them and pick a mild salsa and seasoning. Can/jar sizes are approximate — salsa jars run 16–24 oz.

title: Simply Super Tomatoes

ingredients.prep: firm, medium, peeled and sliced ½-inch thick
chopped with tops, for the dressing

steps: In a small bowl or screw-top jar, combine all the dressing ingredients and mix well.
Arrange the sliced tomatoes in a serving dish (line with fresh lettuce if you wish).
Pour the dressing over the tomatoes.
Cover and chill 1 to 2 hours, basting occasionally.

title: Healthy Doggie Treats
steps: Mash the black beans well (food processor, blender, or a fork). Combine with the peanut butter, banana, and whipped egg and stir until smooth. Cover and refrigerate until chilled and slightly thickened, about 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 325°F. Drop by rounded teaspoon onto a parchment-lined or nonstick pan (makes about 30–34 small biscuits).
After 15 minutes, remove and flatten each with the back of a spoon or a fork. Bake another 10 minutes, then flip the biscuits over.
Continue baking 15–20 minutes (total 40–45 minutes). Cool completely before serving or storing.
notes: 🐶 These are treats for DOGS, not people. For dogs allergic to chicken/eggs, substitute ¼ cup unsweetened applesauce for the egg. Flourless, so they're soft like baked cookies — they freeze well; thaw at room temperature before serving.

title: Beef Bulgogi (Meal Prep)
steps: Combine all the marinade ingredients: sesame oil, gochujang, brown sugar, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, and green onions.
Portion the thinly sliced beef into zip-top bags or freezer-safe containers and divide the marinade evenly among them.
Mix well so the beef is fully coated.
Marinate 1–2 hours before cooking, or freeze immediately (thaw before cooking).
To cook, sear the beef in a hot pan or stir-fry until caramelized and cooked through. Serve with rice or noodles, or toss into a stir-fry.
notes: Per serving: 248 cal, 37g protein, 9g fat, 6g carbs. Freezer-friendly — store up to 3–4 months frozen. The creator notes you can sub 1 tbsp chili crisp if you run out of gochujang, but gochujang is the better option.

title: Bacon & Chive Egg Salad
steps: Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat, then lower to medium and gently lower in the eggs. Cook for 10 minutes.
Transfer the eggs immediately to an ice bath and chill until completely cold. Peel and roughly chop.
Meanwhile, cook the bacon until crisp, drain on paper towels, and chop into small pieces.
Add the cottage cheese and a small splash of water (start with 1 tsp) to a blender and blend until completely smooth and creamy, adding water as needed.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped eggs, bacon, chives, Dijon mustard, and about half the blended cottage cheese.
Season with a pinch of salt and plenty of freshly cracked black pepper. Stir until evenly combined, adding more cottage cheese until dressed to your liking.
For the best flavor, cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.
notes: Blended cottage cheese stands in for mayo to keep it lighter. Scaled for meal prep (a full dozen eggs) — make a half batch if you want less.

title: Lemon Herb Chicken Marinade
steps: Whisk all the ingredients together until combined.

Place about 1 lb (500g) chicken in a freezer bag or container, pour the marinade over, and coat thoroughly. Marinate in the fridge at least 30 minutes, ideally up to 8 hours — or freeze for up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
notes: Makes enough for about 1 lb (500g) of chicken. Great for meal prep — freeze the chicken in the marinade.

title: Yogurt Herb Chicken Marinade
steps: Blend everything with an immersion blender or food processor until smooth.

Place about 1 lb (500g) chicken in a freezer bag or container, pour the marinade over, and coat thoroughly. Marinate in the fridge at least 30 minutes, ideally up to 8 hours — or freeze for up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
notes: Makes enough for about 1 lb (500g) of chicken. Great for meal prep — freeze the chicken in the marinade.

title: Teriyaki Chicken Marinade
steps: Whisk all the ingredients together.

Place about 1 lb (500g) chicken in a freezer bag or container, pour the marinade over, and coat thoroughly. Marinate in the fridge at least 30 minutes, ideally up to 8 hours — or freeze for up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
notes: Makes enough for about 1 lb (500g) of chicken. Great for meal prep — freeze the chicken in the marinade.

title: Mexican Chicken Marinade
steps: Whisk the ingredients until combined.

Place about 1 lb (500g) chicken in a freezer bag or container, pour the marinade over, and coat thoroughly. Marinate in the fridge at least 30 minutes, ideally up to 8 hours — or freeze for up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
notes: Makes enough for about 1 lb (500g) of chicken. Great for meal prep — freeze the chicken in the marinade.

title: BBQ Chicken Marinade
steps: Whisk all the ingredients together.

Place about 1 lb (500g) chicken in a freezer bag or container, pour the marinade over, and coat thoroughly. Marinate in the fridge at least 30 minutes, ideally up to 8 hours — or freeze for up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
notes: Makes enough for about 1 lb (500g) of chicken. Great for meal prep — freeze the chicken in the marinade.

title: Spicy Paprika Garlic Chicken Marinade
steps: Whisk the ingredients until combined.

Place about 1 lb (500g) chicken in a freezer bag or container, pour the marinade over, and coat thoroughly. Marinate in the fridge at least 30 minutes, ideally up to 8 hours — or freeze for up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
notes: Makes enough for about 1 lb (500g) of chicken. Great for meal prep — freeze the chicken in the marinade.

title: BBQ Steak Marinade
steps: Whisk all the ingredients together in a bowl.

Place about 1 lb (500g) steak in a ziploc bag or container, pour the marinade over, squeeze out the air, and seal. Marinate in the fridge 2–24 hours (or freeze up to 3 months; thaw before cooking). Let the steak come to room temperature before cooking.
notes: Makes enough for about 1 lb (500g) of steak (sirloin, ribeye, filet, skirt, or flank). Great for meal prep — freeze the steak in the marinade.
