title: Shrimp Rolls (Lobster Roll Style)

ingredients.prep: about 1.25–1.45 lb, 21/25 count, peeled and deveined
about ½ cup (Duke’s recommended), for the dressing
about ½ cup, finely cut — reserve some for topping
about ½ tsp, for the dressing
about 1 tbsp (~¼ lemon), for the dressing

steps: Make the poaching liquid: combine the water, salt, sugar, and the halved/juiced lemon in a large stock pot and bring to a boil. (Being exact with the water keeps your cook time matching — more water cooks the shrimp faster, less cooks them slower.)
Turn OFF the heat, then gently add the shrimp — this drops the water into a gentle poach (~190–200°F) so they cook slowly and stay tender. Stir once to distribute and poach for 3 minutes.

The shrimp are done when they’ve curled into a "C" (not a tight "O") and are mostly opaque — a little translucency in the very center is fine since they carry over. (For larger 16/20 or 12/15 shrimp, add 30–45 seconds.) Lift them out with a spider onto a paper-towel-lined tray and refrigerate about 20 minutes to stop the cooking and chill.

Cut the cold shrimp into ½-inch ("peanut M&M") pieces — keep them chunky; cutting too small makes it taste like chicken salad. Pat the pieces very dry with paper towel (surface water makes the dressing watery and sloppy).
Gently fold the shrimp together with the mayonnaise, about 15 g of the chives, the salt, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Taste and adjust the salt and lemon.
Toast the buns: heat the oil in a nonstick pan over medium. Open the top-split buns and toast them cut-side down, swirling to coat in the oil, about 90 seconds until golden from tip to tip.

Spread a little mayo inside each bun (a glue so the shrimp stay put), fill with about 3 oz of the shrimp salad (don’t overfill), and top with the reserved chives and a couple cracks of black pepper. Serve immediately.
notes: Make to order — don’t make it ahead; the salt and lemon will "cook" the shrimp and turn the salad weepy. Shrimp: wild Argentinian red shrimp give the most lobster-like sweet, tender result; wild gulf shrimp are the next best; farmed white shrimp work fine (~8.5/10). The same poaching liquid and shrimp also make excellent shrimp cocktail. Optional topping: a little Old Bay (go easy). A fuller version is on brianlagerstrom.com.

title: Sheet Pan Philly Cheesesteak

ingredients.prep: heritage; or sub 24 oz trimmed skirt steak

steps: Slice all the veggies into thin strips on a sheet pan and top with the shaved ribeye (or skirt steak).
Season aggressively with the Worcestershire, steak seasoning, salt, and garlic powder.
Cook in the oven at 425°F for 15 minutes.
Remove, layer the thin provolone over the top, and broil on high 2–3 minutes until melted. Serve.
notes: One-pan, high-protein meal prep. Per serving (4 servings): 331 cal, 46g protein, 10.5g fat, 12g carbs.

title: High-Protein Chocolate Coffee Cheesecake
steps: Add the cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, eggs, cocoa powder, maple syrup, oat (or almond) flour, vanilla, and ground coffee to a blender.
Blend until completely smooth, stopping to scrape down the sides as needed.
Pour the batter into a parchment-lined 6-inch round baking dish and smooth the top.
Bake at 325°F (165°C) for 45–50 minutes, until the edges are set and the center is still soft and slightly jiggly.
Transfer to the fridge and chill at least 8 hours, preferably overnight, before slicing.
notes: High-protein dessert. Use almond flour to keep it gluten-free.

title: Blueberry Cookies

ingredients.prep: frozen blueberries cooked down till thick and jammy — 50g for the dough, 50g to fold in

steps: Make a thick blueberry reduction: cook frozen blueberries down until thick and jammy (you need ~100g). Cool.
Whisk the melted butter, 50g of the blueberry reduction, both sugars, the egg yolk, and the vanilla until smooth.
Fold in the flour, baking soda, and salt.
Gently fold in the white chocolate chunks and the remaining 50g blueberry reduction until just combined.
Scoop onto a parchment-lined baking sheet and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 10–12 minutes, until the edges are lightly golden and the centers still look slightly underbaked.
Let set for 10 minutes before eating.
notes: Thin and chewy inside with a crispy outside.

title: One-Pot French Onion Bolognese

ingredients.prep: for the brown butter parmesan cream
for the tangzhong, plus extra for brushing

steps: Bolognese: cook the sliced onions in the butter, olive oil, and salt until golden and jammy. Remove and set aside.
Brown and season the beef mince, then add the garlic, tomato paste, Worcestershire, thyme, and salt and pepper.
Return the onions, add the plain flour, and cook 1 minute.
Add the beef stock and parmesan rind. Simmer 5 minutes, then add the spaghetti and cook until tender. Remove the rind and stir through the cold butter and red wine vinegar.
Brown butter parmesan cream: brown the 40g butter, then stir in the cream, Dijon, parmesan, and black pepper.

Rolls: cook the 2 tbsp bread flour with the 80ml milk into a thick paste (tangzhong) and cool. Combine the 350g bread flour, yeast, sugar, and salt; add the tangzhong, warm milk, and egg. Mix into a dough, then knead in the softened butter until smooth. Proof 1–2 hours.
Divide the dough into 7 balls, rest 15 minutes, place in a greased dish, and proof 30–45 minutes. Brush with milk and bake at 180°C (350°F) for 15–18 minutes.
Brush the baked rolls with the garlic butter (melted butter, grated garlic, chives, salt, pepper), top with the grated Gruyère, and grill/broil until golden.
To serve, spoon the parmesan cream onto a plate, top with the bolognese, and serve with the rolls.
notes: "Boujee on a Budget" — comes to about $8 per serving.

title: One-Pot Soy-Glazed Chicken & Rice
steps: Thinly slice the cucumbers and pat them dry.
In a bowl, mix the chicken thighs with the salt, black pepper, soy sauce, oyster sauce, honey, and minced garlic.
Wash the rice.
In a rice cooker, add the uncooked rice, chicken bone broth, and marinated chicken, and cook for 30 minutes.
Toss the sliced cucumbers with salt and let sit 15 minutes to draw out the excess liquid.
Squeeze out the liquid, rinse the cucumbers with water, and pat dry.
Toss the cucumbers with the rice vinegar, brown sugar, soy sauce, and sesame seeds to make the salad.
Once cooked, serve half the rice, half the chicken, and half the cucumber salad per bowl.
notes: Per serving: 42g protein, 76g carbs, 8g fat, 567 cal. Makes 2 servings.

title: Seared Scallops with Sweet Corn
steps: Corn pudding: cut the kernels off the cob and pass them through a juicer (or blend and squeeze through a towel to extract the juice). Cook the juice in a pot over medium-low heat until thickened.

Chorizo oil: combine the smoked paprika, chili powder, cumin, coriander seed, crushed garlic, rosemary, and neutral oil in a pot. Gently simmer over low heat 10–15 minutes, then cool completely and strain through a fine mesh lined with cheesecloth.

Corn monté: bring the water to a simmer in a pot, drop the heat to low, and whisk in the cold butter a tablespoon at a time. Once incorporated, stir in a few tablespoons of the corn pudding and season with salt to taste.
Sear the scallops, then serve them over the corn monté, drizzled with the chorizo oil.
notes: The chorizo oil and corn pudding can be made ahead.

title: Vietnamese Shaking Beef

source.note: @bp.ham on TikTok (A–Z of Vietnamese Cooking: Thịt Bò Lúc Lắc)

ingredients.prep: for the marinade, plus more for searing

steps: Cut the steak into 1-inch cubes. In a bowl, marinate with the garlic, chicken powder, sugar, salt, soy sauce, fish sauce, oyster sauce, oil, and black pepper. Mix well and marinate at least 20 minutes (up to a day).
Cut the onion into 1-inch cubes.

Heat a pan over high heat with a little oil. Add the beef spaced apart so it browns (work in batches if needed — you want a sizzle and a hard sear). Sear, then flip and shake the pan; once well-seared, remove and set aside.
Bring the pan to medium heat. Add the onions and a pinch of salt with a splash of water to release the browned bits, then a little oyster sauce. Cook the onions to your liking.
Return the beef, add the butter, and shake/toss until the butter melts and coats everything glossy.
Make the dipping sauce: stir the salt and pepper together and squeeze in lime juice to taste.
Plate over lettuce and tomatoes with rice, and serve with the lime-salt-pepper dipping sauce.
notes: Amounts here are standard estimates — the creator showed the method but did not give measured quantities, so adjust to taste. New York strip, ribeye, or filet all work. Marinate at least 20 minutes (up to overnight). "Lúc lắc" means "shaking" — keep the beef moving in a hot pan.

title: Strawberry Pretzel Pie

ingredients.prep: hulled — about 1 lb to cook, ½ lb fresh
slurried with a splash of water

steps: Crust: mix the crushed pretzels, melted butter, and 3 tbsp sugar. Press into a pie dish and bake at 350°F (175°C) about 8–10 minutes. Cool.

Strawberries: in a saucepan, cook about 1 lb chopped strawberries with the ½ cup sugar, lemon zest, and the cornstarch slurry until thick and jammy. Cool, then stir in the remaining ½ lb fresh chopped strawberries. (No jello — the cornstarch does the work.)
Filling: whip the heavy cream to stiff peaks. Separately beat the cream cheese, ½ cup sugar, vanilla, and lemon juice until smooth, then fold in the whipped cream.
Spread the cream cheese filling over the cooled crust, then spoon the strawberry mixture over the top.
Chill at least 8 hours (so the layers set and adhere) before slicing.
notes: Amounts here are standard estimates — the creator showed the method but did not give measured quantities, so adjust to taste. A no-jello revamp of the Midwestern strawberry pretzel dessert, made as a pie. The fuller version is on the creator’s site.

title: Greek Lemon-Oregano Dressing
steps: Whisk all the ingredients together in a bowl (or shake them in a jar) until combined.
Let it sit 10–15 minutes before serving so the garlic and oregano bloom.
notes: Bright and acidic — great on steak salad and any Greek salad. Makes about ½ cup.

title: Greek Steak Salad

ingredients.prep: sirloin or flank, grilled and sliced

steps: Cook the orzo per the package, drain, and cool slightly.
Season the steak with salt and pepper, grill to your liking, rest, then slice.
Char the zucchini, bell pepper, and red onion on the grill or in a hot pan until blistered, then chop.
Make a batch of the Greek Lemon-Oregano Dressing (see that recipe).
Toss the orzo, charred vegetables, olives, capers, and fresh herbs with most of the dressing.
Top with the sliced steak and drizzle with the remaining dressing. Serve.
notes: Amounts here are standard estimates — the creator showed the method but did not give measured quantities, so adjust to taste. Built around @foodbymaria’s Greek Lemon-Oregano Dressing (added as its own recipe in your box). The video showed the components but not amounts; the full version is in her cookbook, "Greek Out."

title: Girard's Champagne Vinaigrette Dupe

ingredients.prep: canola, or half canola / half mild olive

steps: Dissolve the dry ingredients into the vinegars: in a jar or bowl, whisk the sugar, salt, MSG, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper into the champagne vinegar and white wine vinegar until the sugar and salt dissolve.
Whisk in the white wine, water, Dijon, Worcestershire, and molasses until smooth and slightly thickened from the mustard.
Stream in the oil: while whisking hard (or with a blender running), slowly drizzle in the neutral oil. The Dijon pulls it into a loose, pourable dressing.
Stir in the dried chives. Cover and chill at least 1 hour so it comes together — it tastes noticeably better after resting.
Shake well before each use. Keeps about 2 weeks refrigerated.
notes: A pantry-friendly copycat of Girard's champagne vinaigrette, built around real champagne vinegar. Why the vinegar split matters: champagne vinegar leads for that clean, slightly floral tang; a splash of white wine vinegar adds a sharper edge so it isn't one-note; the dry white wine rounds it out with a winey depth. Dijon does the emulsifying (the bottle uses xanthan gum), so a good shake holds it together. Worcestershire plus MSG carry the savory backbone. Tuning: too sharp → add ½ tsp sugar; tastes flat → nudge MSG and Worcestershire up ¼ tsp each; want it thinner → whisk in water 1 tsp at a time.

title: Lemon Rolls with Cream Cheese & Lemon Curd

ingredients.prep: ¾ cup, for the lemon sugar
½ cup, to pour over the risen rolls

steps: Proof the yeast: stir the warm water, yeast, and 1 tbsp sugar together and let rest 10 minutes until foamy.

Make the dough: mix the flour, melted butter, ⅓ cup granulated sugar, the whisked egg and egg yolk, salt, lemon zest, and the yeast mixture for about 5 minutes. Oil your hands, transfer the dough to a bowl, cover, and let rise 1–2 hours.

Lemon curd: in a saucepan, whisk the sugar, lemon zest, eggs, egg yolk, lemon juice, and butter. Cook over medium heat until slightly thickened, removing from the heat as soon as it just starts to bubble. Cover with plastic wrap touching the surface and refrigerate.
Cream cheese filling: beat the softened cream cheese with 1 tbsp sugar until smooth.
Lemon sugar: rub the ¾ cup granulated sugar together with 1 tbsp lemon zest until fragrant.

Assemble: roll the dough out into a large rectangle. Spread with the cream cheese filling, sprinkle evenly with the lemon sugar, and dot/spread with some of the lemon curd. Roll up into a log, cut into rolls, and place in a greased baking dish.
Cover and let the rolls rest 30 minutes. Pour the heavy cream over the risen rolls.
Bake at 350°F (177°C) for 30–35 minutes, until golden brown.
Frosting: beat the cream cheese, butter, powdered sugar, vanilla, lemon juice, and milk until smooth. Spread over the warm rolls.
notes: The softest lemon rolls, stuffed with cream cheese, lemon sugar, and lemon curd. The roll-up assembly is the standard cinnamon-roll method (shown in the video). Save any extra lemon curd to serve on top.

title: Restaurant-Style Ragù (The Chef Way)

ingredients.prep: added in two stages — about 150 ml early to tenderize the meat, and a ~50 ml splash at the end for roundness

steps: On low heat, add olive oil and the pancetta to a heavy pot and slowly render the fat — don’t brown it; you want the smoky fat to melt into the vegetables.
Add the onion, carrot, and celery with a pinch of salt. Sweat gently (about 10 minutes) until soft, pale, and sweet with no colour — a dark soffritto makes the finished sauce bitter.
Grate in the garlic for the final 1–2 minutes, then remove the soffritto and set aside.
Return the pot to high heat with a little neutral oil. Add the beef mince, breaking it up well, and cook hot to drive off the moisture.

Once the liquid has cooked off, lower the heat slightly and caramelise the beef slowly until deeply browned (like a steak — about 80% of the flavour comes from here), scraping the fond off the bottom regularly.
Add the diced pork belly and gently warm it through — no heavy browning; you mainly want its collagen and fat to enrich the sauce.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook until it darkens and caramelises to a deep red-brown.
Lower the heat and add about 150 ml of the milk (the first stage); let it fully absorb into the meat to soften the fibres.
Deglaze with the red wine, raise the heat slightly, and reduce by about half, scraping the pan clean.
Return the soffritto, then add the passata, stock, parmesan rind, bay leaves, and a pinch of nutmeg.
Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook in a low oven at 120°C (250°F) for 4½ hours, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, make a gastrique: boil the balsamic vinegar and sugar together briefly until lightly thickened.
Once cooked, remove the bay leaves and parmesan rind.
Finish with the remaining ~50 ml milk, a few knobs of cold butter for gloss, and the gastrique added gradually to balance the acidity. Season to taste.
notes: A technique-driven "chef way" ragù using French/European principles (inspired by Marco Pierre White & Massimo Bottura) — soffritto kept soft and sweet, deep Maillard browning on the meat, two-stage milk, and a gastrique to balance. Quick weeknight version: skip the pork belly, pancetta, and wine and just caramelise good mince over a simple soffritto. In the video it’s served over quick gnocchi: bake red-skin potatoes at 160°C until tender, rice them (~500g), and work in 1 egg yolk and 65g "00" flour; roll into ropes, cut, and boil in salted water until they float.

title: Melissa Clark's Best Buttermilk Pancakes

ingredients.prep: 6 tbsp, melted and cooled, plus more for frying
grapeseed, sunflower, or avocado, for frying

steps: Optional: to keep the pancakes warm as you go, heat the oven to 200°F. Otherwise just serve them as you fry.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk the eggs and honey (or sugar) until well combined, then whisk in the buttermilk, melted butter, and the vanilla or zest if using.
Pour the buttermilk mixture into the flour and stir gently with a wooden spoon until just combined — a few lumps are fine. Don’t overmix, or you’ll pop the air bubbles and get flatter pancakes.
Let the batter rest at room temperature at least 10 minutes (up to 30) so the flour hydrates and the batter thickens — this keeps the pancakes from running in the pan.

Heat a skillet or griddle over medium-high until a drop of water sizzles right away, about 30 seconds. Add a drizzle of oil and a little butter and let the butter melt into the oil (this keeps it from burning) — a thin layer of fat, deep enough to swirl. When the foam subsides (about 1 minute), pour ⅓ cup of batter per pancake, leaving space between each.

Cook until bubbles form all over the surface and the edges begin to set, 2 to 3 minutes. If using mix-ins, sprinkle them on now (after the crust forms, so they don’t sink or burn). Flip and cook until golden, 1 to 2 minutes more.
Serve immediately, or keep warm on a rack/baking sheet in the 200°F oven. Add fresh oil and butter to the pan between batches. Serve with salted butter and maple syrup.
notes: The perfect pancakes — crisp edges, fluffy centers. Two leaveners do the work: baking powder for fluff, and baking soda to neutralize the buttermilk’s tang while adding rise and color (use half as much soda as powder). Buttermilk substitutes: plain yogurt + milk, whole milk + lemon juice or vinegar, or sour cream — any acidified dairy reacts with the baking soda. Flour swaps: replace up to ¾ cup of the AP flour with whole wheat, spelt, rye, or oat flour (or ¾ cup/65g oatmeal, or ¾ cup/110g polenta/cornmeal — coarse = crunchy, fine = cakier). Make-ahead: batter keeps 2 days in the fridge (a touch less fluffy), or mix everything but the leaveners ahead and stir those in ~10 min before cooking; cooked pancakes freeze well (reheat in the toaster ~3 min, +30 sec to crisp). Pan: cast iron, carbon steel, or stainless give crispier edges; nonstick is easiest to flip; never cook on high or the bottom burns before the center sets. Mix-ins (sprinkle on, don’t stir in): blueberries, sliced strawberries or banana, diced peaches, chocolate or butterscotch chips, dried cherries, chopped nuts, sprinkles, coconut, or poppy/sesame seeds.

title: Dan Dan Udon Noodles

ingredients.prep: or creamy peanut butter, for the sauce
1–2 tbsp (Lao Gan Ma or your favorite), for the sauce

steps: Combine all the sauce ingredients in a bowl, mix well, and set aside.
Boil water and cook the udon noodles through; blanch the bok choy for 30 seconds to 1 minute. Strain and set aside.

Heat 1 tbsp cooking oil and sauté the minced garlic about 10 seconds until fragrant. Add the ground meat and cook ~80% through, then season with the hoisin, dark soy, five spice, and chicken broth. Bring to a boil and cook the meat through.
Turn off the heat, add the cooked strained noodles and the sauce, and toss well.
Serve topped with the bok choy, roasted peanuts, extra chili oil, and lots of thinly sliced green onion.
notes: Serves 4–5.

title: Crispy Garlic Parmesan Chicken Wraps
steps: Cut the chicken breast and thighs into cubes. Add the seasonings and grated parmesan with a drizzle of olive oil and mix until well coated.

Make the sauce: in a pan on low heat, melt the light butter with the Italian herbs, garlic powder, and paprika and toast about 1 minute. Add the evaporated milk, parmesan, and cream cheese and stir until smooth and creamy.

Line a sheet pan with parchment, spread the marinated chicken evenly (don’t overcrowd), spray a light layer of cooking spray, and bake in a preheated oven at 200°C (400°F) for 20–22 minutes until crispy.
While the chicken is still hot, toss with extra parmesan and parsley until coated and cheesy.
Assemble each wrap with shredded lettuce, onion, tomato, the crispy chicken, and the creamy sauce. Enjoy.
notes: Makes 10 wraps. Per wrap: 423 cal, 41g protein, 25g carbs, 18g fat. Storage: keep the chicken and sauce in separate containers in the fridge; reheat chicken in the oven/air fryer 4–5 min at 180°C/370°F and warm the sauce in a pan before serving.

title: Loaded Chicken and Potato Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C). Coat a 9- x 1-inch baking dish with cooking spray.
Combine potatoes, olive oil, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, black pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt in a large bowl; toss until evenly coated. Transfer to the prepared baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven until potatoes are golden and fork-tender, 25 to 30 minutes. Set aside.

Meanwhile, cook bacon in a large skillet over medium, turning occasionally, until crisp, 10 to 15 minutes. Drain bacon on a paper towel-lined plate; remove and reserve 1 tablespoon drippings from the skillet. When bacon is cool enough to handle, finely chop, and set aside (about 1/2 cup).
In the same large bowl, combine chicken, cream cheese, water, scallions, hot sauce, 1/4 cup of the bacon pieces, reserved bacon drippings, 1 1/2 cups cheese, and remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt.
Fold chicken mixture into the potatoes in the casserole until well combined. Sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup shredded cheese.
Bake until cheese is melted, 10 minutes longer.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and switch to the oven's broiler function. Broil on high for 3 minutes or until cheese is bubbling and brown in spots.
Garnish with remaining bacon (about 1/4 cup) and additional scallions. Serve with sour cream.

title: Million Dollar Chicken Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
Stir cream of chicken soup, cottage cheese, sour cream, cream cheese, Creole seasoning, onion powder, and 1/2 teaspoon of the garlic powder in a medium bowl until well blended and smooth.
Fold in chicken and parsley until evenly coated.
Combine crackers, melted butter, and remaining 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder in a medium bowl.
Spoon chicken mixture evenly into an 11x7-inch or 9-inch square baking dish. Sprinkle evenly with mozzarella then top with cracker mixture.
Place on a foil-lined baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven until crackers are golden brown and edges are bubbly, about 35 minutes. Garnish with scallions, if desired.
notes: This dish will keep in the freezer for up to 4 months. To reheat, thaw overnight or up to 6 hours in the refrigerator, then bake according to directions. To change up the flavor, substitute different types of rotisserie chicken, like mojo, lemon pepper, or garlic. You can use just about any crisp butter cracker. Feel free to add peas or steamed broccoli.

title: Broccoli Chicken Casserole

ingredients.item: condensed cream of mushroom soup (such as Campbell's)

steps: Gather all ingredients.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).

Place chicken breasts in a large pot; add enough water to completely cover chicken. Bring to a gentle simmer; cover tightly with a lid and cook until chicken is no longer pink in centers, about 15 minutes.
Transfer chicken to a cutting board. Cut into bite-sized pieces when cool enough to handle.
Combine condensed soup and mayonnaise in a bowl.
Place chicken into a 9x13-inch baking dish; layer cooked broccoli, soup mixture, and Cheddar cheese over top.
Sprinkle dry stuffing mix over top.
Bake in the preheated oven until bubbly and cheese has melted, 25 to 30 minutes.
Serve hot and enjoy!
notes: Use more cheese and an extra can of soup for a creamier version. You can use fresh or frozen broccoli; some reviewers use cream of chicken soup instead of cream of mushroom.

title: Cheesy Chicken and Broccoli Casserole
steps: Gather ingredients, preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C), and butter the bottom and sides of a 9x13-inch dish.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add chicken and continue to boil until no longer pink, 5 to 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine broccoli, cream of chicken soup, Cheddar cheese, 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese, mozzarella cheese, sour cream, and pepper in a large bowl. Mix well.
Drain chicken and add to broccoli mixture. Mix well. Pour into the prepared baking dish and spread evenly.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes. Add remaining Parmesan cheese on top and continue to bake until cheese is melted, 3 to 5 minutes.
notes: You can use thawed, frozen broccoli instead of fresh if preferred. After baking for 20 minutes, you can sprinkle whatever cheese you like on top. Eat as-is or serve over rice.

title: King Ranch Chicken Casserole

ingredients.item: diced tomatoes with green chile peppers (such as Ro*tel)

ingredients.prep: 3-pound, torn into shreds or cut into chunks, divided

steps: Gather the ingredients. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add onion and bell peppers; sauté until they begin to soften, about 2 minutes. Transfer to a large bowl.

Stir mushroom and chicken condensed soups, diced tomatoes with chile peppers, chicken broth, sour cream, cumin, ancho chile powder, oregano, and chipotle chile powder into onion mixture until sauce is well combined.

Reserve 1/2 cup sauce and 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese for topping. Spread a few tablespoons of the remaining sauce into a 9x13-inch baking dish. Top with 1/2 of the chicken, 1/2 of the remaining sauce, 1/2 of the remaining Cheddar cheese, and 1/2 of the tortillas.

Top tortillas with the remaining chicken, remaining sauce, remaining Cheddar cheese, and remaining tortillas. Spread the reserved 1/2 cup sauce over the tortillas and sprinkle with the reserved 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese.
Bake casserole in the preheated oven until bubbling, about 40 minutes.
Turn on the broiler; broil until the top is golden, 2 to 3 minutes more.
Serve hot. Enjoy!
notes: You can add a poblano for extra heat, or use Pepper Jack instead of Cheddar. Some reviewers use Doritos or tortilla chips instead of corn tortillas for a crunchy twist. Any type of cooked chicken works, including rotisserie chicken.

title: Chicken Pot Pie Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Lightly grease a 9x13-inch casserole dish.
Melt butter in a large Dutch oven over medium. Add onion and cook, stirring often, until tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir in flour, thyme, mustard, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper; cook, stirring constantly, 1 minute.

Gradually stir milk and broth into flour mixture; cook, stirring constantly, until bubbling and thickened, 3 to 5 minutes. Stir in chicken, vegetables, and parsley. Remove from heat, and cover to keep warm.
Unroll crescent dough sheets onto a cutting board. Cut each dough sheet crosswise into 12 (1-inch) strips to get 24 strips total. Roll each strip to form a spiral.
Pour chicken mixture into prepared casserole dish; arrange dough spirals on top of filling.
Bake in the preheated oven until filling is bubbling around edges and crescent dough is golden brown, 20 to 25 minutes. Let stand, uncovered, for 15 minutes before serving.
notes: Recipe developed by Laura Kanya. Additional standing time: 15 minutes.

title: Creamy Chicken Cordon Bleu Casserole
steps: Gather all ingredients.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Lightly grease a 9x13-inch casserole dish.

Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook egg noodles in boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, 7 to 9 minutes. Drain and transfer noodles to the prepared casserole dish.
Top noodles with chicken, ham, and Swiss cheese.
Mix together condensed soup, milk, and sour cream in a medium bowl until combined; spoon over noodle mixture in the casserole dish.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat; cook and stir bread crumbs and Parmesan cheese in hot butter until crumbs are well coated, 2 to 3 minutes. Sprinkle over casserole.
Bake in the preheated oven until casserole is bubbling and lightly browned, about 30 minutes.
Allow to cool slightly before serving.

title: Quick and Easy Green Chile Chicken Enchilada Casserole
steps: Gather the ingredients. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Lightly grease a medium baking dish.

Season chicken with garlic salt; arrange in the prepared baking dish. Bake in the preheated oven until no longer pink and juices run clear, about 45 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 165 degrees F (74 degrees C).
Cool chicken, shred with two forks, and set aside.
Meanwhile, char each tortilla half over the open flame of a gas stove burner using metal tongs until lightly puffed, about 1 minute.
Pour about 1/2 inch enchilada sauce into the bottom of a medium baking dish; arrange 6 tortillas in a single layer on top.
Sprinkle with 1/2 of the chicken, 1/3 of the cheese, 1/2 of the sour cream, and 1/3 of the remaining enchilada sauce.
Repeat layers. Coat remaining tortillas thoroughly with some of the remaining enchilada sauce and arrange on top. Sprinkle with remaining cheese and top with any remaining enchilada sauce.
Cover and bake in the preheated oven for 45 minutes.
Cool slightly before serving.
notes: If you want to skip the charring step, simply tear the tortillas into quarters so they absorb the sauce better.

title: Chicken Alfredo Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
Place chicken into a pot and cover with water. Bring to a boil over high heat; reduce heat and simmer until no longer pink in the centers and juices run clear, about 15 minutes.
At the same time, bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add penne and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 11 minutes. Drain.
Drain cooked chicken and cut into bite-size pieces.
Place chicken, noodles, Alfredo sauce, 1/2 mozzarella cheese, milk, onion powder, and garlic powder in a large bowl and mix well.
Pour into a 1 1/2-quart casserole dish. Layer remaining mozzarella cheese and Cheddar cheese on top.
Bake in the preheated oven until melted and bubbly, about 25 minutes.
notes: You can use any medium pasta noodles that you prefer, such as ziti, large elbow, etc. If using the second half of a jar of Alfredo sauce, swirl the milk in the jar to get all of it out.

title: Chicken Noodle Casserole
steps: Gather all ingredients. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).

Fill a large pot with water and bring to a simmer over medium heat. Add chicken; poach in simmering water until no longer pink in center, about 12 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to remove chicken from pot and set aside. Leave water in the pot.
Bring chicken cooking water to a boil. Stir in egg noodles and cook until tender with a slight bite, about 10 minutes. Drain.
Transfer noodles to a large bowl. Cut chicken into bite-size pieces and mix with noodles.

Mix together both condensed soups and sour cream in a separate bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Add soup mixture to chicken mixture and gently stir until combined. Transfer to a 2-quart baking dish.
Melt butter in a small saucepan over low heat and remove from heat. Stir in crumbled crackers.
Sprinkle mixture over top of casserole.
Bake in the preheated oven until heated through and browned on top, about 30 minutes.
notes: Leftovers are delicious topped with melted cheese.

title: Chicken and Stuffing Casserole

ingredients.prep: 6 ounce, such as Stove Top, or more to taste
stalks, diced, or more to taste
whole, cooked, skinned, boned, and shredded

steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with cooking spray.

Bring water and 1/4 cup butter to a boil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add stuffing mix and stir to combine. Remove the saucepan from the heat, cover, and let sit until water is absorbed, about 5 minutes. Fluff stuffing with a fork.
While you are preparing stuffing, melt 1 tablespoon butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and celery; cook and stir until softened, 5 to 10 minutes.
Mix both condensed soups with sour cream in a bowl until well combined.
Spread shredded chicken in the bottom of the prepared baking dish. Layer onion mixture over chicken and season with salt and pepper.
Top with soup mixture and spread stuffing evenly over top.
Bake in the preheated oven until filling is bubbly and topping is golden brown, about 1 hour.
Serve hot and enjoy!
notes: If using leftover chicken, aim for about 4 cups of shredded chicken. Leftover turkey at Thanksgiving works well, too. Add frozen peas or mixed vegetables for a heartier meal. Leftovers reheat beautifully for easy lunches.

title: Mamaw's Chicken and Rice Casserole
steps: Gather all ingredients. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Grease sides and bottom of a casserole dish.
Stir chicken, water, rice, cream of chicken soup, cream of celery soup, and cream of mushroom soup together in the prepared casserole dish; season with salt and pepper.
Arrange butter evenly over the top of the chicken mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until the rice is tender and the chicken is cooked through, 60 to 75 minutes.
Cool 10 to 15 minutes before serving. Enjoy!

title: Chicken and Spinach Casserole

ingredients.prep: 10 ounce each, thawed and squeezed dry

steps: Gather all ingredients.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) and arrange rack in the upper third position. Coat a 9x13-inch baking dish with cooking spray.

Melt butter in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, Italian seasoning, crushed red pepper, and 3/4 teaspoon of salt; cook, stirring often, until onions are softened and translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute. Gradually add milk, and cook, stirring constantly, until slightly thickened, about 3 minutes.

Add cream cheese, and remaining 1 teaspoon salt; cook, stirring constantly, until fully combined. Remove from heat and stir in shredded chicken, cooked rice, spinach, lemon juice, and lemon zest until fully combined. Transfer mixture to prepared baking dish and spread in an even layer. Sprinkle evenly with mozzarella and Parmesan.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, about 15 minutes. Turn on the broiler and broil until cheese is browned, 2 to 3 minutes.

title: Chicken Cobbler

ingredients.prep: 11.36 ounce, such as Red Lobster Cheddar Bay Biscuit Mix

steps: Gather the ingredients. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Place butter in the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish and place in the oven to melt.
When butter is melted, add chicken in a single layer over the butter. Top evenly with frozen veggies.

Stir together biscuit mix, seasoning packet from the mix, and milk in a bowl until well blended; pour over the chicken and veggies. Spread lightly and evenly over the top of the mixture but do not stir.
Whisk together soup and stock in a second bowl until smooth. Pour over biscuit mix but do not stir.
Bake in the preheated oven uncovered until very golden brown and only very slightly jiggly in the center, about 45 minutes.
Sprinkle parsley over the top if desired and let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
notes: Inspired by the viral TikTok recipe from Matthew Bounds (@yourbarefootneighbor).

title: Buffalo Chicken and Roasted Potato Casserole

ingredients.prep: shredded, such as Great Value Fiesta Blend

steps: Gather all ingredients.
Preheat the oven to 500 degrees F (260 degrees C). Spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with cooking spray.
Heat hot pepper sauce, olive oil, garlic powder, black pepper, paprika, and salt in a large skillet over low heat, stirring until thoroughly combined. Turn off heat.
Toss potatoes in batches with the hot pepper sauce mixture to coat and use a slotted spoon to transfer potatoes to the prepared baking dish. Leave remaining sauce in the skillet.
Mix chicken into remaining sauce and allow to marinate while potatoes roast.
Bake potatoes until tender inside and crisp and brown outside, 45 to 50 minutes, stirring every 10 to 15 minutes.
Reduce oven heat to 400 degrees F (205 degrees C).
Spread chicken cubes over roasted potatoes.
Sprinkle shredded cheese, cooked bacon, and green onions over chicken. Return to the oven.
Bake in oven until chicken is cooked through and the cheese topping is bubbling, about 15 minutes.

title: Chicken Enchiladas with Creamy Green Chile Sauce
steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Fry tortillas (one at a time) for 5 seconds on each side to soften and make them pliable. Add more oil to the pan as needed.
Drain between layers of paper towel and keep warm.
Divide chicken, 10 ounces Monterey Jack cheese, and onion among 12 tortillas.
Roll up each tortilla and place seam-side down in the prepared pan.

Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add flour and whisk until mixture begins to boil. Slowly add chicken broth, stirring with a whisk until thickened. Mix in sour cream and green chiles, stirring occasionally. Heat thoroughly but do not boil.
Pour mixture over enchiladas.
Bake in the preheated oven until bubbly and heated through, about 20 minutes. Top with remaining Monterey Jack cheese and bake for 5 more minutes.
Garnish with chopped green onions and cilantro.

title: Pesto Chicken Penne Casserole
steps: Preheat an oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish. Combine the bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, and olive oil in a small bowl until evenly moistened; set aside.

Fill a large pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Once the water is boiling, stir in the penne, and return to a boil. Cook the pasta uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the pasta has cooked through, but is still firm to the bite, about 11 minutes. Drain well in a colander set in the sink.

Meanwhile, combine the chicken in a bowl with the Italian cheese blend, spinach, tomatoes, alfredo sauce, pesto sauce, and milk. Stir in the pasta once done, and scoop into the prepared baking dish. Top with the bread crumb mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until bubbly and golden brown on top, 40 to 45 minutes.
notes: Cook's Note: I make my own Alfredo sauce and use 2 cups in place of the jarred sauce.

title: Spaghetti Chicken Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, 10 to 12 minutes.

Drain spaghetti and transfer to a large bowl. Add chicken, 1 1/2 cups Cheddar cheese, condensed soup, onion, pimentos, bell pepper, wine, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined, then spread mixture into a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes. Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup Cheddar over casserole and bake until melted, about 15 minutes longer.

title: No-Peek Chicken
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish with cooking spray.
Whisk rice, water, chicken soup, mushroom soup, onion, bell pepper, onion soup mix, and oregano in a large bowl until well combined.
Pour rice mixture into the prepared dish.
Sprinkle chicken thighs with seasoning salt and arrange on top of rice mixture. Cover dish tightly with foil.
Bake in the preheated oven, keeping tightly covered, for 1 hour 30 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 165 degrees F (74 degrees C).
Serve and enjoy.
notes: Test Kitchen Tip: This recipe was tested in our test kitchen in May 2023 and updated to bake, covered, for 1 hour and 30 minutes. We used 2 cups of water instead of chicken broth and added onion soup mix. Called 'no-peek' because the foil traps steam and keeps the chicken tender and juicy — resist the urge to lift the foil while baking.

title: Chef John's Chicken and Rice Casserole

ingredients.prep: or as needed, for the sauce

steps: Add yogurt, lime zest and juice, salt, paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne, white pepper, cardamom, cinnamon, and allspice for marinade to a large mixing bowl; whisk to combine.

Make one or two slits into each piece of dark chicken meat, down to the bone. Add chicken parts to the yogurt marinade and toss very thoroughly. Wrap in plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator for 4 to 8 hours.
Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C). Lightly grease a 9x13-inch casserole dish.
Grind saffron in a mortar with a pestle. Pour in 1/4 cup chicken broth and stir to combine.

Combine unsalted butter and salt in a saucepan; pour in remaining 2 cups chicken broth and chicken broth-saffron mixture. Bring to a boil over high heat. Add basmati rice and stir to combine. Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and let simmer gently for exactly 15 minutes; do not disturb while cooking. Turn off the heat and let rest for 10 minutes.

While rice is still hot, transfer into the prepared casserole dish. Use a fork to fluff the rice while gently spreading into an even layer. Place the chicken pieces, skin-side up, on top of rice. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with salt.

Roast in the center of the preheated oven until chicken is no longer pink in the centers and juices run clear, about 45 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh, near the bone, should read 165 degrees F (74 degrees C).

While chicken is in the oven, mix yogurt, garlic, green onions, mint, cilantro, salt, and water together for sauce. Reserve in the refrigerator until needed. Serve chicken and rice on a plate and top with sauce.
notes: Chef's Notes: I used sheep's milk yogurt, but any plain, full-fat yogurt will do. If you don't have white pepper, you can use freshly ground black pepper. You can use any long-grain white rice. The page URL says 'exotic chicken and rice casserole'; the recipe's current title is 'Chef John's Chicken and Rice Casserole'. Total time is long because of the 4-8 hour marinade.

title: Make-Ahead Chicken Divan Casserole

ingredients.prep: shredded cooked, or more to taste

steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
Combine Cheddar cheese and 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese in a small bowl and set aside.
Place a steamer insert into a saucepan and fill with water to just below the bottom of the steamer. Bring water to a boil. Add broccoli, cover, and steam until tender, 2 to 6 minutes.
Meanwhile, melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add mushrooms, onion, and garlic; saute until soft, 5 to 7 minutes.

Whisk together condensed soup, sour cream, milk, and mayonnaise in a large bowl until smooth. Add 1/2 cup Cheddar-Parmesan mixture and season with salt and pepper. Add sauteed vegetables and stir to combine sauce.

Spread 1/2 cup cheese-vegetable sauce onto the bottom of a 9x13-inch casserole dish. Add cooked rice as the bottom layer. Add steamed broccoli as the next layer, then top with a layer of cooked chicken. Cover with remaining cheese-vegetable sauce. Pat down evenly and smooth with a spatula.
Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining Cheddar-Parmesan mixture, plus extra Parmesan if desired, and continue to bake until browned, about 15 minutes more.
notes: Cook's Notes: You can use cooked brown rice instead of white, cubed cooked chicken instead of shredded, and cream of chicken soup instead of mushroom. If you like a really browned top, put it under the broiler for 2 to 3 minutes after baking for 30 minutes. This can be prepared a day or two in advance and baked before serving.

title: Pantry Chicken Casserole
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Coat a 9x13-inch casserole dish with cooking spray.

Fill a large pot of lightly salted water and bring to a roiling boil. Stir in penne and return to a boil. Cook pasta uncovered, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 11 minutes; drain.

While pasta is cooking, melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper; cook, stirring occasionally, until tender, about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms; cook until their liquid is released, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until fragrant, about 1 minute.

Stir in cheese, diced tomatoes with juice, diced tomatoes and green chiles with juice, and chile peppers; cook and stir until cheese melted, 5 to 10 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in cooked pasta and chicken then pour into the prepared baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven until heated through, about 20 minutes. Serve warm.
notes: Tips: You can substitute margarine for butter, any shelf-stable processed cheese food for Velveeta, and two (12.5 ounce) cans of cooked, flaked chicken for leftover chicken. You can use two (4 ounce) cans of sliced mushrooms instead of fresh mushrooms; add them when you add the tomatoes. Choose hot Rotel and peppers if you like heat.

title: Cheap Chicken Noodle Casserole
steps: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Spread cooked egg noodles in a 9x13 inch baking dish and set aside.

In a large skillet over medium high heat, saute cubed chicken meat with onion for about 7 to 10 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and no longer pink inside. Add the soup, milk, peas and carrots and stir together. Season with salt and pepper, paprika and chili powder to taste. Stir until just bubbly and remove from heat.
Stir chicken mixture into noodles in baking dish until well combined; top with cheese slices, then bread cubes.
Bake at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for about 30 minutes or until bread is toasted; serve hot.

title: The Best Parmesan Chicken Bake
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
Coat the bottom of a 9x13-inch casserole dish with olive oil, and sprinkle with garlic and red pepper flakes.
Arrange chicken breasts in the bottom of the dish.
Pour marinara sauce over chicken. Sprinkle basil over marinara sauce, and top with 1/2 of the mozzarella cheese, followed by 1/2 of the Parmesan cheese, and croutons.
Top with remaining mozzarella cheese and remaining Parmesan cheese.

Bake in the preheated oven until cheese and croutons are golden brown and the chicken is no longer pink inside, about 35 minutes to an hour, depending on the shape and thickness of your chicken breasts. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of a chicken breast should read at least 160 degrees F (70 degrees C).
Serve hot and enjoy!
notes: Recipe Tips: You can use more marinara sauce if you like. Fake Parmesan will not taste the same. Go buy some real Parmigiano-Reggiano, please!

title: Ladybug's Poppy Seed Chicken Casserole

ingredients.prep: skinless, boneless, cooked and cut into bite-sized pieces

steps: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).

Mix together the cracker crumbs, melted butter, and poppy seeds in a bowl. Layer the bottom of a 9x13-inch glass baking dish with 2/3 of the cracker mix. Mix the chicken breast pieces with cream of chicken soup and sour cream until thoroughly combined, and spoon the mixture on top of the crumb crust. Sprinkle remaining 1/3 of the cracker mix over the top of the chicken mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until the casserole is hot and bubbling, about 1 hour.
notes: Cook's Notes: To freeze, prepare as above. Let cool then wrap pan with foil and freeze. To cook after freezing, let thaw in fridge overnight and cook for 1 hour at 350 degrees F. If not thawed, cook until warmed through. It is even better after being frozen.

title: Classic Marinara Sauce
steps: Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the sliced garlic (and red pepper flakes, if using) and cook gently until fragrant and just golden, 1–2 minutes — do not brown it.
Add the crushed tomatoes, salt, and sugar. Bring to a simmer.
Simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, 25–30 minutes, until thickened and the raw-tomato taste is gone.
Stir in the torn basil and taste for salt. For a smoother sauce, blend with an immersion blender.
notes: Makes about 4 cups — enough to sauce 1 lb of pasta with some left over. Freezes well.

title: Baked Mac and Cheese
steps: Heat the oven to 375°F. Cook the macaroni in well-salted water 1 minute short of al dente, then drain.
Melt 4 tbsp of the butter in a large pot over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the warm milk and cook, whisking, until thickened, 4–5 minutes.
Off the heat, stir in 3 cups of the cheddar, the salt, pepper, and ground mustard until smooth.
Fold in the drained macaroni. Transfer to a greased 9x13-inch baking dish and top with the remaining 1 cup cheddar.
Melt the last 1 tbsp butter, toss it with the panko, and sprinkle over the top.
Bake 25–30 minutes, until bubbly and golden. Rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: For extra-creamy, use half cheddar and half Gruyère or Monterey Jack.

title: Simple Roast Chicken
steps: Heat the oven to 425°F. Pat the chicken very dry and, if you have time, let it sit at room temperature about 30 minutes.
Rub the softened butter all over the chicken (and under the breast skin), then season generously inside and out with the salt and pepper.
Stuff the cavity with the garlic, lemon halves, and thyme. Tie the legs together with kitchen twine and tuck the wing tips under.
Place breast-side up on a rack in a roasting pan (or in a cast-iron skillet). Roast 70–80 minutes, until the thickest part of the thigh reads 165°F and the juices run clear.
Rest 15 minutes before carving.
notes: Save the carcass for stock. A squeeze of the roasted lemon over the carved meat is lovely.

title: Easy Fried Rice

ingredients.prep: day-old and cold, jasmine or long-grain

steps: Heat 1 tbsp of the oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the beaten eggs, scramble until just set, and remove to a plate.
Add the remaining 2 tbsp oil. Add the garlic and green onion whites and stir 30 seconds, then add the peas and carrots and cook 2 minutes.
Add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry 3–4 minutes until heated through and starting to crisp.
Push the rice to the side, add the soy sauce and sesame oil, then toss everything to coat and return the eggs.
Stir in the green onion greens, season with salt and pepper, and serve.
notes: Cold, day-old rice is the secret — fresh rice turns mushy. Add cooked chicken, shrimp, or any leftover protein if you like.

title: Guacamole
steps: Halve the avocados, remove the pits, and scoop the flesh into a bowl. Add the lime juice and salt and mash to your desired texture.
Fold in the red onion, tomato, cilantro, and the jalapeño and garlic if using.
Taste and adjust with more lime or salt. Serve right away with tortilla chips.
notes: Use ripe Hass avocados that yield to gentle pressure. To store, press plastic wrap directly onto the surface to slow browning.

title: Philly Cheesesteak Beef Marinade

ingredients.prep: thinly sliced (or sirloin for a cheaper option)
to melt into the sandwiches when cooking

steps: Combine all the marinade ingredients with the beef in a freezer bag or container and coat well. Marinate in the fridge, or freeze up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
When ready, cook in a hot pan or on the grill until done to your liking.
notes: Meal-prep marinade. When cooking, melt provolone over the beef and peppers and pile onto rolls.

title: Rosemary Garlic Butter Beef Marinade

ingredients.prep: or flank/skirt, cubed or cut into strips against the grain
cut into squares and frozen; add when the steak is almost done cooking

steps: Combine all the marinade ingredients with the beef in a freezer bag or container and coat well. Marinate in the fridge, or freeze up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
When ready, cook in a hot pan or on the grill until done to your liking.
notes: Meal-prep marinade. Freeze the butter squares in the bag and drop them in near the end of cooking for a glossy garlic-butter finish.

title: Carne Asada Beef Marinade

ingredients.prep: or flank/skirt, cut into strips against the grain

steps: Combine all the marinade ingredients with the beef in a freezer bag or container and coat well. Marinate in the fridge, or freeze up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
When ready, cook in a hot pan or on the grill until done to your liking.
notes: Meal-prep marinade. Great in tacos, burritos, or bowls.

title: Korean Stir-Fry Beef Marinade

ingredients.prep: or flank/skirt, cut into strips against the grain

steps: Combine all the marinade ingredients with the beef in a freezer bag or container and coat well. Marinate in the fridge, or freeze up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
When ready, cook in a hot pan or on the grill until done to your liking.
notes: Meal-prep marinade. Serve over rice with kimchi and green onion.

title: Beef Shawarma Marinade

ingredients.prep: or flank/skirt, cut into strips against the grain

steps: Combine all the marinade ingredients with the beef in a freezer bag or container and coat well. Marinate in the fridge, or freeze up to 3 months (thaw before cooking).
When ready, cook in a hot pan or on the grill until done to your liking.
notes: Meal-prep marinade. Serve in pita or over rice with garlic sauce and pickles.

title: Tiramisu (Chef Dennis)

ingredients.prep: Savoiardi style, about 1½ of the 7 oz packages

steps: Add 1 inch of water to a small pot and bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Set a metal bowl on top so it sits on the pot without touching the water.
Add the egg yolks and sugar to the bowl and whisk to combine.

Set the bowl over the simmering water and whisk constantly for about 10 minutes to cook the eggs — the mixture will thicken and grow in volume (this is the sabayon). It should reach at least 165°F. If it gets too hot, lift the bowl off for 15 seconds while continuing to whisk.
Off the heat, keep whisking briefly to cool it down. It should be thick and lemon-yellow. Let it cool slightly.
Add the room-temperature mascarpone to the yolks and mix just until combined — don’t overmix, or it can curdle.
In a separate bowl, whip the heavy cream to stiff peaks, starting slow and increasing the speed over about 10 minutes (this keeps it stable). Use very cold, fresh cream.
Gently fold the whipped cream into the mascarpone mixture and refrigerate until you’re ready to assemble.
Mix the cold espresso with the coffee liqueur. Quickly dip the ladyfingers into the mixture just long enough to wet them — do not soak.
Arrange a layer of dipped ladyfingers in the bottom of a 9-inch square dish.
Spread half the mascarpone cream evenly over the ladyfingers.
Add a second layer of dipped ladyfingers, then spread the remaining cream on top.
Refrigerate at least 4 hours (overnight is best). Dust with cocoa powder just before serving.
notes: No raw eggs — the yolks are cooked in the sabayon. Use full-fat mascarpone (Galbani is great) and very cold, fresh heavy cream. Only quick-dip crunchy Savoiardi ladyfingers; cake-style get too soft. Chilling overnight gives the cleanest slices and best flavor. Omit the liqueur (and add a little more espresso) for an alcohol-free version. ~490 cal/serving.
